title: Enabling AI NLP feature in Superset · apache/superset · Discussion #39274 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: How to enable the AI Feature in Apache Superset, when Installed using Docker compose
author: umamaheswararao37
comment_excerpt: Hi @umamaheswararao37 ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. Official Apache Superset does not currently include native AI/NLP or text-to-SQL features [1] . However, there are a couple of paths you can take: Community Fork (most mature option): A community fork at diegoscarabelli/apache-superset-ai (branch diego/development , based on Superset v6.0.0) implements a text-to-SQL assistant in SQL Lab with support for OpenAI, Google Generative AI, and Anthropic [2] . To use it with Docker Compose: Clone the fork Update env_variables.sh : export SUPERSET_GITHUB_URL= " https://github.com/diegoscarabelli/apache-superset-ai.git " export TAG= " diego/development " Configure your LLM provider API keys No special feature flag is needed — it works out of the box [2] Future direction: The Superse || can you please help me with the steps to set up || Since official Apache Superset doesn't have native AI/NLP features yet, here are the steps to set up the community fork ( diegoscarabelli/apache-superset-ai ) which adds text-to-SQL to SQL Lab [1] : 1. Clone the fork and switch to the AI branch: git clone https://github.com/diegoscarabelli/apache-superset-ai.git cd apache-superset-ai git checkout diego/development 2. Configure environment variables — edit docker/.env (or create docker/.env-local to override defaults) [2] : SUPERSET_SECRET_KEY=your-unique-secret-key DATABASE_PASSWORD=your-secure-password SUPERSET_LOAD_EXAMPLES=yes # optional, loads sample data 3. Start Docker Compose: docker-compose up -d This spins up PostgreSQL, Redis, Superset, Celery workers, and Nginx. Access Superset at http://localhost:8088 (default login: admin / ad || i am facing this issue , guide me

title: FILTER SORT ORDER · apache/superset · Discussion #39308 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: FILTER SORT ORDER — Quarters appearing alphabetically This is a known Superset issue but has workarounds. Create a calculated sort column in the underlying dataset (e.g., a numeric quarter_order field) and use the Sort Metric configuration in the filter to sort by that column while displaying the quarter label. This is a data modelling fix, not a platform limitation. i need to sort the month not in alphabetical order but in chronologically order ... in superset 5. @dosubot
author: srijani-smsrc
comment_excerpt: You're on the right track with the approach you described! Superset's native filters have a built-in Sort Metric feature that handles exactly this. Here's how to get months sorted chronologically: Add a numeric sort column to your dataset — Create a calculated column (e.g., month_order ) that maps each month to its numeric value (January → 1, February → 2, … December → 12). You can do this in your database view/query or as a calculated column in the Superset dataset. For example in SQL: EXTRACT(MONTH FROM your_date_column) AS month_order . Configure the filter's sort settings — In your dashboard's native filter configuration panel, expand the "Sort filter values" section [1] . There you'll find: Sort type — Set to ascending [2] Sort Metric — Select your month_order column here [3] This tel || @dosu how to do this in dashboard filters. || Here's how to set it up directly in the dashboard filter UI: Step 1 — Add a calculated column to your dataset Go to Datasets , find your dataset, and click Edit . Go to the Calculated Columns tab [1] . Click "+ Add item" and create a column called month_order with a SQL expression like: EXTRACT(MONTH FROM your_date_column) Or if your months are text labels: CASE WHEN month_name = ' January ' THEN 1 WHEN month_name = ' February ' THEN 2 WHEN month_name = ' March ' THEN 3 WHEN month_name = ' April ' THEN 4 WHEN month_name = ' May ' THEN 5 WHEN month_name = ' June ' THEN 6 WHEN month_name = ' July ' THEN 7 WHEN month_name = ' August ' THEN 8 WHEN month_name = ' September ' THEN 9 WHEN month_name = ' October ' THEN 10 WHEN month_name = ' November ' THEN 11 WHEN month_name = ' December ' THEN 1

title: HAMBURGER MENU ICONS · apache/superset · Discussion #39307 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: HAMBURGER MENU ICONS (my feedback) This requires frontend code customisation in Superset's UI layer. It's not trivial but it's absolutely possible — Superset is open-source and we control the deployment. Flag this for a future sprint if not immediately feasible. In place of the hamburger button, specific icons for the options (Force Refresh, Fullscreen & Download) should be there which files do i need to change in ui layer and is this recommended at all? @dosubot
author: srijani-smsrc
comment_excerpt: Yes, this is doable — it's a common UI customization. Here are the key files you'd need to touch: Primary files to modify: superset-frontend/src/dashboard/components/SliceHeaderControls/index.tsx — This is the main file. It renders the kebab menu (three vertical dots via Icons.EllipsisOutlined ) inside a NoAnimationDropdown , and defines all the menu items: Force Refresh, Fullscreen, Download, etc. [1] . You'd replace the dropdown with individual icon buttons and wire each to the existing action handlers already in handleMenuClick [2] . superset-frontend/src/dashboard/components/SliceHeader/index.tsx — The parent component that renders the header-controls section containing SliceHeaderControls . You may need to adjust its layout to accommodate inline icon buttons instead of a single dropdo || is this recommended ? yes or no , keep in mind maintenance and the effort it will take to implement this. @dosubot || No , I wouldn't recommend it for most teams. Here's why: Maintenance cost is high — you're forking core dashboard UI behavior. Every time you upgrade Superset, you'll need to check and re-apply your changes to SliceHeaderControls and SliceHeader , which are actively maintained components. Implementation effort is moderate — the code change itself isn't huge (2-3 files), but the Download action is a submenu with multiple export formats, so you can't just replace it with a single icon. You'd still need a dropdown or popover for that one, which adds complexity. Space constraints — chart headers have limited horizontal room. Adding multiple icons can crowd the header, especially on smaller screens or when charts are arranged in dense dashboard layouts. If this is purely a UX preference and not

title: question · apache/superset · Discussion #39306 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: This is non-negotiable for a client-facing BI tool. Superset has supported Drill By and Drill to Detail since v2.1+. The DRILL_BY and DRILL_TO_DETAIL feature flags need to be enabled in the Superset config. Additionally, dashboard-to-dashboard navigation using URL parameters can simulate hierarchical drill-through (Company → BU → Division). Please check if these feature flags are enabled in our deployment. If not, enable them and test. is this correct ? what i want to do is that i have a table company the moment i click on a simple company , that same table chart will become bu chart of that company , and when i click a particular bu , that same chart will become division chart under that bu @dosubot
author: srijani-smsrc
comment_excerpt: The suggestion you received is partially correct , but what you're describing — clicking a company in a table and having that same table automatically transform into a BU chart filtered to that company, then clicking a BU to see divisions — is not exactly how Superset's Drill By works . Here's the reality: What Drill By actually does: When you right-click a data point in a chart, you select "Drill by" from the context menu, then manually choose a new dimension column. This opens a modal overlay (not an in-place replacement of the chart) showing aggregated data grouped by the new dimension, filtered to your selection. It supports breadcrumb navigation so you can drill through multiple levels [1] . However, it's an ad-hoc exploration tool — you must manually pick the next dimension each time

title: datetime data type shown as string · apache/superset · Discussion #39271 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi team, After upgrading Apache Superset from 4.1.4 to 6.0.0, we are seeing an issue with one date/datetime column in a dataset. Issue One column that was previously usable for time filtering is no longer being recognized correctly as a temporal column after the upgrade. Another date/datetime column in the same dataset still works fine as temporal and can be set as the default datetime column. But for the affected column: in the dataset column list it shows ? instead of a proper type icon when we try to use it for time filtering, Superset generates SQL that fails this was not happening before the upgrade in 4.1.4 What we checked reviewed the dataset in the dataset editor one date/datetime column is marked as temporal and works correctly another date/datetime column is present in the dataset but appears to have unknown or unrecognized type metadata we suspect either: metadata or type inference changed between 4.1.4 and 6.0.0 dataset column metadata needs refresh/sync Oracle type handling changed in Superset 6 with cx_Oracle Environment Superset version before upgrade: 4.1.4 Superset version after upgrade: 6.0.0 Database: Oracle Driver: cx_Oracle Questions Has temporal column type inference changed in Superset 6 for Oracle datasets? Why would one Oracle date/datetime column appear as unknown (?) after upgrade while another date/datetime column in the same dataset works? Is there any migration step, metadata sync step, or dataset refresh needed after upgrade for temporal columns? Are there any known Oracle-related issues in Superset 6 around datetime columns and time filters? Is the recommended fix to: sync columns from source recreate the dataset cast the column in SQL / virtual dataset or update Oracle driver/dialect configuration?
author: asishPatnaik2000
comment_excerpt: This is a recognized pattern after upgrading Superset across major versions with Oracle. Here's what's likely happening and how to fix it: Why one column shows "?" and the other doesn't: Superset determines if a column is temporal by regex-matching the database type string (e.g., ^date , ^timestamp ) reported by the driver [1] . If the driver returns a NULL, empty, or non-standard type string for a column, it won't match any pattern and will show as unknown (?). The Oracle engine spec has no custom type mappings — it relies entirely on these default patterns [2] . So if one column's type string is being reported differently by the driver after upgrade, that column will lose its temporal status while others continue to work. What changed between 4.x and 6.0.0: Driver migration from cx_Oracl || @dosu is it a driver issue? || Probably not a driver issue. Superset 6.0.0 still actually depends on cx_Oracle 8.0.x in its requirements — only the documentation was updated to recommend oracledb [1] [2] . So unless you manually switched drivers, you're still on the same driver as before. The more likely cause is changes in how Superset detects and handles Oracle temporal columns between 4.x and 6.0.0. Specifically: Several PRs tightened temporal column detection, replacing heuristic fallbacks with stricter actual-type checks (PRs feat(datasets): add datetime format detection to dataset columns #36150 , fix(timeshift): Add a more reliable strategy for correct temporal col #36309 ). If the affected column's type string wasn't being reported cleanly by SQLAlchemy's inspector, it may have passed the old looser checks but n || @dosu is there any issue here I am getting database connection issie return type cant conver to 0 || The error "return type can't convert to 0" doesn't appear in any Superset code or issues I can find — it's most likely coming from the cx_Oracle or oracledb driver itself , not Superset. Superset's Oracle engine spec doesn't implement any custom type handlers or converters; it relies entirely on the driver and SQLAlchemy's Oracle dialect for type conversion [1] . A few things to check: Driver setup : Superset 6.0.0 still depends on cx_Oracle 8.0.x under the hood [2] . If you're using oracledb instead, make sure you've aliased it properly in your config with sys.modules["cx_Oracle"] = oracledb [3] . A misconfigured driver can cause unexpected type conversion errors. Connection string format : Verify you're using the correct format — oracle://user:password@host:1521/?service_name=YOUR_SERVIC

author: villelachute
comment_excerpt: Hi @villelachute ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. The main reason your UI is still in English is that the official Docker images don't include compiled translation files by default — they're excluded to reduce image size [1] . Setting BABEL_DEFAULT_LOCALE alone isn't enough. Here's what you need to do: 1. Rebuild your Docker image with translations enabled: docker build --build-arg BUILD_TRANSLATIONS=true -t superset:custom . Or in your Docker Compose setup, pass the build arg so that both backend .mo and frontend .json translation files get compiled [2] . 2. Add a bootstrap override to your superset_config_docker.py : There's a known issue in Superset (versions 5.0.0–6.0.0) where a race condition causes the frontend to render in English before the language pack loads [3] . title: Glimvia - Apache Superset on Mobile (iOS and Android) · apache/superset · Discussion #39178 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hey Superset community, I built something I wanted to share with you all. It is called Glimvia, a mobile app that brings your Superset dashboards to your phone on both iOS and Android. The problem it solves : Superset is a great tool but it is not optimized for mobile. Decision-makers often need to check KPIs on the go and the browser experience on a phone is painful. Glimvia fixes that. What it does : It gives you touch-optimized dashboards built for mobile screens, lets you connect to your existing Superset instance without any auth changes, and sends push notifications for alerts so you never miss a timely decision. Core features are free and setup takes just a few minutes. How it works : You point it at your Superset URL, authenticate once, and your dashboards are live in the app. No backend changes, no new infrastructure, nothing to reconfigure on the Superset side. Available on iOS and Android. More at glimvia.app. Would love feedback from anyone who has wanted a proper mobile experience for Superset. Happy to answer questions.

title: does SQL_FORMAT_QUERY = False suppoted in superset 6.0.0 released version? · apache/superset · Discussion #39158 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: does SQL_FORMAT_QUERY = False suppoted in superset 6.0.0 released version?
author: asishPatnaik2000
comment_excerpt: @dosu please check does SQL_FORMAT_QUERY = False suppoted in superset 6.0.0 released version? || SQL_FORMAT_QUERY is not a supported configuration option in Apache Superset — it does not exist in the current codebase and there's no evidence it was ever part of official Superset . So it is not supported in version 6.0.0. That said, Superset does have SQL formatting functionality: There's a "Format SQL" button in SQL Lab that uses sqlglot for query pretty-printing . A REST API endpoint at /format_sql/ is available for programmatic formatting . However, there is no global config toggle to enable/disable SQL formatting. Could you share where you saw SQL_FORMAT_QUERY referenced? It may be from a custom fork or third-party documentation. If you're trying to control a specific SQL formatting behavior, I can help you look into the right approach. To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? G || @dosu I want to fix this error from it Oracle Error oracle error: ORA-00979: not a GROUP BY expression This may be triggered by: Issue 1002 - The database returned an unexpected error. how to fix it? updated the sqlglot version in superset pod to sqlglot==27.29.0 || This is a well-documented issue — Oracle does not support positional GROUP BY (e.g., GROUP BY 1, 2, 3 ), but Superset via SQLGlot generates exactly that, causing the ORA-00979 error [1] . Upgrading sqlglot to 27.29.0 (which you've already done) is the recommended fix and has worked for other users [2] . One user confirmed it resolved their issue completely . If you're still seeing the error after the upgrade, try these additional steps: Restart your Superset pod to ensure the new sqlglot version is fully loaded. Disable the "Formatted" option in SQL Lab — this prevents Superset from rewriting your queries with positional GROUP BY [1] . Check which context triggers the error — it can happen in SQL Lab, chart queries, or native filters. Native filters in particular trigger a subquery wrappin

author: cabsil
comment_excerpt: Hi @cabsil ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. Hi Christophe, The issue is that the docker-bootstrap.sh script doesn't support an "mcp" argument - it only handles worker , beat , app , and app-gunicorn . The MCP service also requires a separate ASGI server (uvicorn) and can't run within Flask's application context, which is why you're seeing that error. To run the MCP server in Docker: Enable MCP in your Superset configuration: ENABLE_MCP_SERVICE = True Modify your container command to run MCP directly instead of using docker-bootstrap.sh: command : ["superset", "mcp", "run", "--port", "5008"] Or create a custom entrypoint script that runs uvicorn with the MCP service. The MCP service is designed to run as a separate ASGI process outside the Flask application , not as part of the || I think Dosu is giving information about the previous implementation and not what's in 6.1.0rc1. Any other advice ? Thanks || The Dosu answer above is partially outdated. In 6.1.0rc1, the MCP server setup changed. Here's what actually works: The error No application found. Either work inside a view function or push an application context means the Flask app context isn't being initialized before the MCP server tries to access SQLAlchemy/database resources. You need to make sure the app context is pushed before the MCP service starts. Try this approach: Option 1: Use the CLI command with proper environment superset_mcp : env_file : - path : docker/.env required : true - path : docker/.env-local required : false image : *superset-image container_name : superset_mcp_app command : ["python", "-c", "from superset.app import create_app; app = create_app(); app.app_context().push(); from superset.mcp.server import run_m || based on the option 1, I just copy and paste into the docker-compose-image-tag.yml when I try start the docker, it keep restarting for the "superset_mcp_app"

author: joserocha
comment_excerpt: This is a known limitation with how Superset processes SQL queries differently between SQL Lab and charts. Why it happens: When creating charts, Superset wraps queries in subqueries to apply limits and other transformations. SQL Server's OPTION clauses must appear at the end of the outermost query, so when your query gets wrapped, the OPTION (MAXDOP 1) ends up inside a subquery where it's syntactically invalid. This is part of a broader pattern affecting T-SQL features since Superset adopted sqlglot for SQL parsing in 4.1.0+. Workarounds: Create a database view - Encapsulate your query with the OPTION hint in a SQL Server view, then query that view from Superset charts Set MAXDOP at the database level - Configure query governor settings in SQL Server itself rather than per-query hints Use  || Any updates on this … On Sat, Mar 14, 2026, 8:55 AM dosubot[bot] ***@***.***> wrote: *dosubot[bot]* left a comment ( apache/superset#38650 ) #38650 (comment) > This is a known limitation with how Superset processes SQL queries differently between SQL Lab and charts. *Why it happens:* When creating charts, Superset wraps queries in subqueries to apply limits and other transformations. SQL Server's OPTION clauses must appear at the end of the *outermost* query, so when your query gets wrapped, the OPTION (MAXDOP 1) ends up inside a subquery where it's syntactically invalid. This is part of a broader pattern affecting T-SQL features #31913 > since Superset adopted sqlglot for SQL parsing in 4.1.0+. *Workarounds:* 1. *Create a database view* - Encapsulate your query with the OPTION hint in a S || Going to move this over to a discussion. || The Dosu explanation is correct about the root cause -- Superset wraps your virtual dataset query in a subquery, and OPTION (MAXDOP 1) can only appear at the outermost level of a SQL Server statement. It gets buried inside the subquery and SQL Server rejects it. The database view workaround is the most practical path, but there's a subtlety: you cannot put OPTION hints inside a view definition either. SQL Server doesn't allow query hints in views. What you can do: Use a stored procedure + virtual dataset -- Create a stored proc with the hint, then query it from SQL Lab: CREATE PROCEDURE dbo . sp_my_report AS BEGIN SELECT col1, col2, col3 FROM my_table WHERE ... OPTION (MAXDOP 1 ) END Then in SQL Lab: EXEC dbo.sp_my_report and save as virtual dataset. The catch is that Superset won't try to || This issue is likely related to how Apache Superset wraps the query internally when generating charts. In SQL Server, the OPTION (MAXDOP 1) query hint must appear at the end of the final SELECT statement. However, Superset wraps the query inside a subquery (virtual table), as seen in the log: SELECT TOP 50 OPTION (MAXDOP 1) ) AS virtual_table Because of this wrapping, the OPTION clause is no longer at the valid position, which results in the syntax error: "Incorrect syntax near the keyword 'OPTION'" Possible Workarounds Remove OPTION (MAXDOP 1) from the query when using Superset charts This is the simplest approach if parallelism is not critical Use a database-level or server-level configuration for MAXDOP This avoids the need for query-level hints Create a view or stored procedure in SQL

Thanks, Hariprasath N … On Thu, 22 Jan 2026 at 17:34, dosubot[bot] ***@***.***> wrote: *dosubot[bot]* left a comment ( apache/superset#37357 ) #37357 (comment) > The log message "Loaded your LOCAL configuration at [/app/docker/pythonpath_dev/superset_config.py]" means Superset is running with its default Docker config because no custom override was found or loaded. When you created superset_config_docker.py and "superset init" failed, it was likely due to a syntax or import error in that file—Superset only catches ImportError, so any other error will cause startup to fail and show a traceback in t || Let's dig deeper since some config is now picked up but emails still aren't sent. Here’s what will help pinpoint the issue: Please share (with sensitive info redacted) the relevant parts of your superset_config_docker.py : the FEATURE_FLAGS , CeleryConfig , and all SMTP_* settings. After restarting, do your logs show Loaded your Docker configuration at [...] ? If not, the override isn’t loaded. In your worker container, run env | grep ALERT_REPORTS_NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN and confirm it is False . If this is True in the environment, emails will never be sent—even if the config file says False ( source ). Check your Celery worker and beat logs for lines mentioning reports.scheduler , reports.execute , or any errors around the time a report should trigger. If you never see reports.execute , the || Hi Dosu, This is my config file # Feature flags FEATURE_FLAGS = { "ALERT_REPORTS": True, "PLAYWRIGHT_REPORTS_AND_THUMBNAILS": True, } ALERT_REPORTS_NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN = False # Celery configuration from celery.schedules import crontab class CeleryConfig: broker_url = "redis://redis:6379/0" result_backend = "redis://redis:6379/0" worker_concurrency = 4 beat_schedule = { "reports.scheduler": { "task": "reports.scheduler", "schedule": crontab(minute="*", hour="*"), }, "reports.prune_log": { "task": "reports.prune_log", "schedule": crontab(minute=10, hour=0), }, } # Playwright / Chromium (for screenshots) SUPERSET_FEATURE_PLAYWRIGHT_REPORTS_AND_THUMBNAILS = True # SMTP configuration SMTP_HOST = "smtp.yourmail.com" SMTP_STARTTLS = True SMTP_SSL = False SMTP_USER = ***@***.***" SMTP_PORT = 587 || Hello, this appears to be a Q&A request for help rather than a bug report. I would suggest for starters running an official release docker image like apache/superset:6.0.0 . I can verify that Alerts & Reports work great with a docker compose deployment and that version, so this is a matter of your configuration rather than a bug in Superset. Feel free to work with Dosu, there's also the #ask-ai channel in Superset Slack. But I am inclined to convert this to a discussion, though, as it doesn't need to be in our bug tracker. If, when you get this resolved, you find there's something we should add to the docs, we'd love if you could add it!

title: Enhancing the AI Assistant (Kapa) UI/UX on superset.apache.org · apache/superset · Discussion #39061 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I’ve been using the "Ask AI" feature on superset.apache.org and noticed that while the functionality is great, the interface feels quite rudimentary and contains several visual/UX hurdles. the current implementation feels stylistically disconnected from the modern Apache Superset brand. I am proposing a UI/UX revamp to make the assistant feel more integrated, visually clear, and more consistent with the aesthetics of superset current problems: -Visual Bugs: Certain elements, such as the send button, have background color conflicts that make them nearly invisible to the user. -Accessibility: Lack of robust support for dark/light mode switching and high-contrast elements. -Zero Message Distinction: The user's "hello!" and the AI's response are just lines of text. There’s no "bubble," no background color change, and no alignment difference (like right-aligning the user). It feels like reading a notepad, not having a conversation. -Awkward Suggestion Buttons: Those "How do I install..." buttons are massive but mostly empty space. On a high-end UI, these should be smaller, side-by-side chips or cards. -"Deep Thinking" Placement: That button is just floating there. It feels like an afterthought rather than a feature of the input. While the AI assistant is a third-party widget from Kapa.ai, it is highly extensible. Kapa’s documentation confirms that the widget can be styled and customized to our needs. I am deeply interested in seeing this through and would like to be actively involved in the implementation of these changes. I have experience with Vue, React, and modern CSS/UI design, and i hope that I can provide the style updates to achieve a more proper/updated look for the modal.
author: MouhibKhammassi

author: Dhruvin4530
comment_excerpt: @dosu Could you please help? || The issue is in how you're selecting the ChromeDriver version. Your script picks the last known-good version that matches the Chrome major version, but that's not guaranteed to match the exact patch version of google-chrome-stable . This is exactly why you ended up with Chrome 146.0.7680.177 but ChromeDriver 146.0.7680.165 . Instead of matching on just the major version, you should match on the full version (or at least the major.minor.build prefix like 146.0.7680 ). Here's a corrected approach: RUN wget https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \ && apt install -y --no-install-recommends ./google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \ \ && CHROME_VERSION=$(google-chrome --version | awk '{print $3}' ) \ && CHROME_BUILD=$(echo $CHROME_VERSION | cut -d '.' -f 1-3)  || This code is still giving the same. 12 15.60 Google Chrome 146.0.7680.177 #12 15.61 ChromeDriver 146.0.7680.165 (4b989da09e15a7dc0de0785cb5ff232aadae3f0f-refs/branch-heads/7680@{#2932}) #12 DONE 15.8s || @dosu || @dosu Here is my dockerfile, I am switching to playwright. =1.10.8, =0.1.4" pysaml2 statsd # Update to the hydrolix rebased clickhouse-sqlalchemy to be compatible with new Superset version RUN uv pip install git+https://github.com/sbdelisle/clickhouse-sqlalchemy@hdx-0.1.0 "pyathena>=1.10.8, =0.7.2" "cryptography>=42.0.4, FROM apache/superset:5.0.0 AS test # Switching to root to install the required packages USER root RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y # pysaml2 requirement RUN apt-get install git xmlsec1 wget unzip jq -y # Installing playwright & chromium RUN uv pip install playwright \ && playwright install --with-deps chromium # https://lxml.de/installation.html # RUN pip install lxml # Example: installing the MySQL driver to connect to the metadata database # if you prefer Postgre || The Dockerfile looks good for installing Playwright and Chromium. A couple of things to make sure of: 1. Add the config flag in your superset_config.py to actually enable Playwright: PLAYWRIGHT_REPORTS_AND_THUMBNAILS = True Without this, Superset will still try to use Selenium even though Playwright is installed [1] . 2. Optional tuning — you can adjust timeouts if needed [2] : SCREENSHOT_PLAYWRIGHT_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 60000 # milliseconds SCREENSHOT_PLAYWRIGHT_WAIT_EVENT = "load" # or "networkidle" for heavier dashboards 3. Memory — make sure your worker container has at least 1–2 GB of memory . Chromium will silently fail to start if resources are too low [3] . 4. Quick sanity check — after building, you can verify Playwright works inside the container by running: playwright screenshot htt || @dosu This is not sending full screenshot || It looks like the screenshot is being cut off because the dashboard content exceeds the configured viewport height. Superset captures a specific HTML element within the browser viewport — it does not scroll or dynamically measure the full page height [1] . So anything taller than your configured window size gets truncated. A few things to try: 1. Increase the dashboard viewport height — Your current WEBDRIVER_WINDOW sets the dashboard viewport to 1600×2000 . If your dashboard is taller than 2000px, increase the height: WEBDRIVER_WINDOW = { "dashboard" : ( 1600 , 4000 ), # increase height to fit your dashboard "slice" : ( 3000 , 1200 ), "pixel_density" : 1 , } 2. Increase load wait times — If some charts haven't finished rendering before the screenshot is captured, they'll appear blank. Inc || @dosu My dashboards are very lengthy in size and I have set "dashboard": (1600, 6000, is there any other option which can fit for all kinds of dashboards? || Since you've already switched to Playwright, the best option for handling dashboards of varying heights is the tiled screenshot feature, which is available in Superset 5.0+ [1] . It automatically divides tall dashboards into multiple viewport-sized tiles and stitches them into a single image — so you don't need to guess a viewport height. It's actually enabled by default , but you can tune the thresholds in your superset_config.py [2] : SCREENSHOT_TILED_ENABLED = True # enabled by default SCREENSHOT_TILED_HEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 5000 # trigger tiling if dashboard is taller than 5000px SCREENSHOT_TILED_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT = 2000 # height of each captured tile With this, you can set your WEBDRIVER_WINDOW dashboard height back to a reasonable value (e.g., (1600, 2000) ), and the tiling logic will auto

title: guest role permissions · apache/superset · Discussion #39023 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: i made a new role for embedding dashboard what permissions i need to give and what cnfigurations i need to set because rigt now i am getting data forbidden error @dosubot
author: srijani-smsrc
comment_excerpt: The "data forbidden" error with embedded dashboards is very common and usually comes down to a few things. Here's what you need: 1. Configuration in superset_config.py FEATURE_FLAGS = { "EMBEDDED_SUPERSET" : True } GUEST_ROLE_NAME = "EmbedGuest" # Your custom role name GUEST_TOKEN_JWT_SECRET = "your-strong-secret-here" # MUST change from default GUEST_TOKEN_JWT_EXP_SECONDS = 300 # Token expiration (5 min default) [1] 2. Minimum Permissions for Your Custom Role Grant these permissions to your "EmbedGuest" role [2] [3] : can read on Dashboard can read on Chart can read on CurrentUserRestApi — this one is critical and often missed; without it the embedded dashboard shows a black screen with "Something went wrong" [3] can explore on Superset can explore_json on Superset can read , can list , c

title: superset 6 permissions · apache/superset · Discussion #39021 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: what are the initial permissions given to pubic role in superset 6 @dosubot
author: srijani-smsrc
comment_excerpt: By default, the Public role in Superset has NO permissions — it starts empty [1] . It only receives permissions when you explicitly set PUBLIC_ROLE_LIKE in your configuration and run superset init . When configured with PUBLIC_ROLE_LIKE = "Public" , the Public role gets these permissions after running superset init [2] : Read access : Dashboard, Chart, DashboardPermalinkRestApi, ExplorePermalinkRestApi, EmbeddedDashboard, Annotation, AnnotationLayerRestApi, CssTemplate Read/Write : DashboardFilterStateRestApi Datasource : can_get , can_external_metadata on Datasource API : can_time_range , can_query_form_data , can_query on Api A few things to keep in mind: Without setting PUBLIC_ROLE_LIKE , anonymous users are always redirected to login [2] . Any manual permission changes made via the UI

title: Hide/remove the "Charts" section in landing page for certain users · apache/superset · Discussion #39019 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I have a multi-tenancy enabled Superset installation. For some external user dashboards, I need to give dashboard view only access. For such users, is it possible to hide/disable the "Charts" widget/section in the landing page? Does this require front end changes? Thanks, Teena @dosu
author: georgeteena
comment_excerpt: Hi Teena, There's currently no built-in configuration or role-based setting to hide the Charts section from the landing page for specific users. The only section that currently respects permissions is Saved Queries , which hides itself if the user lacks can_read on that resource [1] . A community PR ( #33946 ) has been submitted to hide the Charts section based on can_write permissions, but it's still awaiting review [2] . Best workaround today — bypass the welcome page entirely by redirecting users to /dashboard/list/ using FLASK_APP_MUTATOR in your superset_config.py [3] : from flask_appbuilder import expose , IndexView from flask import g , redirect class SupersetDashboardIndexView ( IndexView ): @ expose ( '/' ) def index ( self ): if not g . user : return redirect ( '/login' ) return  || @dosu Code review comment - appbuilder.indexview cannot be overridden like above — FAB registers routes at init time, so post-init mutation has no effect. The before_request hook is the correct mechanism for runtime redirects. What worked: DASHBOARD_ONLY_ROLES = {"role-1", "role-2"} def is_dashboard_only_user(): if not g.user or g.user.is_anonymous: return False user_role_names = {role.name for role in g.user.roles} return bool(user_role_names & DASHBOARD_ONLY_ROLES) def flask_app_mutator(app): @app.before_request def redirect_welcome_page(): # List of welcome page paths to intercept welcome_paths = [ "/", "/superset/welcome/", "/superset/welcome", "/welcome/", "/welcome", ] if request.path in welcome_paths: if hasattr(g, "user") and g.user and not g.user.is_anonymous: if is_dashboard_only || Great catch — you're right that appbuilder.indexview can't be reliably overridden via FLASK_APP_MUTATOR since FAB registers its routes at init time. The before_request hook is the correct approach for runtime redirects. Thanks for sharing the working solution! The role-based check with DASHBOARD_ONLY_ROLES is a clean pattern, and intercepting all the welcome page path variants ensures nothing slips through. This should be very helpful for Teena and anyone else with a similar multi-tenancy setup. To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? title: MapBox - Tool Tip Change for Clarity - Decrease Usability Issues · apache/superset · Discussion #38985 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Issue: There are many standard ways to specify Latitude / Longitude (see list at bottom). Not specifying what format leaves it open for the end user to misunderstand / use incorrectly / and incorrectly make the conclusion that the feature doesn't work when it's just a UI / Clarification issues. Solution: Make clear in tool tips that Latitude/Longitude is Decimal Degrees (DD). Other Latitude / Longitude Formats Decimal Degrees (DD) - 21.3069°, -157.8583° Decimal Degrees, Minutes - 21° 18.42′, -157° 51.50′ Decimal Degrees, Minutes, Seconds (DMS) - 21° 18′ 25.00″, -157° 51′ 30.00″ World Geodetic System (WSG) / EPSG:4326 - Use in GPS, Aviation, Catography, NATO,, etc... ISO 6709:2022 - 50°40′46″N 95°48′26″W 123.45m

title: Map Box ViewPort Improvement - Limit options for Longitude/Latitude · apache/superset · Discussion #38983 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Prevent users from entering in INVALID Latitude / Longitude Decimal Degrees. Currently you can put in any number, including invalid ones. No error message is thrown for invalid numbers. Solution: Limit Latitude Field to -90 to 90 Limit Longitude Field to -180 to 180 Logic: This follows the World Geodetic System (WSG 84) Rules Latitude Range: -90° (South Pole) to +90° (North Pole). Longitude Range: -180° (West) to +180° (East). Hemispheres: In decimal format, North and East are positive (+), while South and West are negative (-). Additional Clarity - Tool Tip: Make clear in tool tips that Latitude/Longitude is Decimal Degrees (DD) and NOT DMS(Decimal Degrees Seconds) or any other format.
author: clhubert

title: Slow saving changes on exist dataset · apache/superset · Discussion #38975 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, why my superset app is so slow when i do some small changes on virtual dataset?
author: FrancescoCastaldi
comment_excerpt: This is a well-documented pain point. When you save a virtual dataset, Superset runs a two-stage synchronous pipeline [1] : UpdateDatasetCommand — saves your metadata changes to the Superset DB (with several validation queries for column/metric uniqueness, existence, etc.) RefreshDatasetCommand — executes your dataset's SQL query (with LIMIT 1 ) against the underlying data warehouse to sync column metadata [2] That second step is the main bottleneck: the HTTP request blocks until your warehouse responds. If you're connected to something like Databricks, Snowflake, or BigQuery with cold-start or query compilation overhead, even a trivial save can take a long time [3] . There's currently no async offloading for this step. A few other factors that can compound the slowness: Large number of co || Superset can be slow with virtual datasets due to complex queries, large data volumes, lack of caching, or resource constraints. Try optimizing queries, enabling caching, and checking backend performance for better speed. … On Tue, Mar 31, 2026, 8:49 AM dosubot[bot] ***@***.***> wrote: This is a well-documented pain point. When you save a virtual dataset, Superset runs a *two-stage synchronous pipeline* [1] https://github.com/apache/superset/blob/829e4d92d91ceae4b43b1ed3b063ffe45377799c/superset/datasets/api.py#L429-L431 > : 1. *UpdateDatasetCommand* — saves your metadata changes to the Superset DB (with several validation queries for column/metric uniqueness, existence, etc.) 2. *RefreshDatasetCommand* — *executes your dataset's SQL query* (with LIMIT 1) against the underlying data wareho

title: Restrict dashboard filter "Owner" and "Modified by" from listing all users · apache/superset · Discussion #38964 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, When you got to dashboards/charts list, the filter "Owner" and "Modified by" lists all users. Is there a way to disable or restrict listing all the users? My use case needs a restriction - only one specific user should be visible, or no users at all. Thanks!
author: georgeteena
comment_excerpt: @dosu || Yes, Superset provides configuration options to restrict which users appear in the Owner and Modified by filter dropdowns. Here are the main approaches: 1. EXCLUDE_USERS_FROM_LISTS — a simple static blocklist in superset_config.py [1] : EXCLUDE_USERS_FROM_LISTS = [ "user1" , "user2" , "unwanted_user" ] 2. EXTRA_RELATED_QUERY_FILTERS — the most flexible option, allowing dynamic SQLAlchemy-based filtering [2] . For example, to show only one specific user : def user_filter ( query , * args , ** kwargs ): from superset import security_manager user_model = security_manager . user_model return query . filter ( user_model . username == "allowed_user" ) EXTRA_RELATED_QUERY_FILTERS = { "user" : user_filter } Or to show no users at all : def user_filter ( query , * args , ** kwargs ): from superset  || Use **EXTRA_RELATED_QUERY_FILTERS** with a custom filter to show only one user or hide all users: ```python def user_filter(query, *args, **kwargs): from superset import security_manager user_model = security_manager.user_model return query.filter(user_model.username == "allowed_user") # Or use user_model.id == None to hide all EXTRA_RELATED_QUERY_FILTERS = {"user": user_filter} ``` Restart Superset after applying. … On Mon, Mar 30, 2026, 10:02 PM dosubot[bot] ***@***.***> wrote: Yes, Superset provides configuration options to restrict which users appear in the *Owner* and *Modified by* filter dropdowns. Here are the main approaches: *1. EXCLUDE_USERS_FROM_LISTS* — a simple static blocklist in superset_config.py [1] https://github.com/apache/superset/blob/829e4d92d91ceae4b43b1ed3b063ffe453

title: World Map: Hover highlight does not reset — countries with no data stay colored after mouse-out #37569 · apache/superset · Discussion #38961 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I want to fix in docker image currently im using superset3.1.2 image. how do i fix this issue
author: Nivraj
comment_excerpt: This is a known bug ( #37569 ) that affects Chrome and Edge (specifically Chromium 145+) — Firefox is unaffected [1] . The root cause is a conflict between custom mouseover / mouseout handlers and Datamaps' built-in highlightOnHover behavior, where two competing restore paths race each other and the highlight gets stuck [2] . There have been several fix attempts: PR #37716 and PR #38030 were merged but the bug still persists in Chrome/Edge 145+. PR #38297 and PR #38471 propose the definitive fix (removing the custom handlers entirely and relying on Datamaps' built-in hover), but neither appears to be merged yet [2] . Since you're on Superset 3.1.2 , none of these fixes are in your version. Your options: Patch the file manually in your Docker image — Edit superset-frontend/plugins/legacy-pl

title: Superset 6.0 Embedded Dashboard: /api/v1/me/roles/ API returns 403 Forbidden · apache/superset · Discussion #37406 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Bug description Switch Superset code to branch 6.0 Run Superset using docker compose up --build, Superset works fine at this point Open my project and use @superset-ui/embedded-sdk to embed a Dashboard When running my project and opening the embedded dashboard, the Superset API endpoint /api/v1/me/roles/ returns 403 Forbidden. Screenshots/recordings Superset version 6.0 Python version 3.11 Node version 18 or greater Browser Chrome Additional context No response Checklist I have searched Superset docs and Slack and didn't find a solution to my problem. I have searched the GitHub issue tracker and didn't find a similar bug report. I have checked Superset's logs for errors and if I found a relevant Python stacktrace, I included it here as text in the "additional context" section.
author: arthurworkspace
comment_excerpt: Hi @arthurworkspace ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. The 403 Forbidden error on /api/v1/me/roles/ when embedding dashboards in Superset 6.0.0 is usually caused by stricter authentication and permission checks introduced in this version. The endpoint now requires a valid JWT guest token with the correct claims, and the embedding setup must be properly configured. Common causes include: missing or misconfigured guest token (wrong dashboard id/uuid, secret, audience, or expired token), dashboard not published or not enabled for embedding, missing allowed_domains for your embedding domain, or CORS/X-Frame-Options misconfiguration. Also, the dashboardId must be present in all form_data sent to the backend, and any row-level security rules should be reviewed for misconfiguration. Re || Hi, I attempted to reproduce this issue on the 6.0 branch but got different results. I logged in as a user with the Gamma role and accessed /api/v1/me/roles/. In my environment, I received a 200 OK response with the expected role data, rather than a 403 Forbidden. Could you please check your configuration, specifically the exact permissions assigned to your restricted user? It appears my local 'Gamma' role might inherently have can_read on User or Security by default, which is masking the issue on my end. That said, looking at the code in superset/security/api.py, I do see the @permission_name("read") decorator, which implies that the endpoint is indeed enforcing a global read check. I agree that removing this decorator is likely the correct fix, but I wanted to confirm the permission set  || We had a similar issue. Fix: rollback to 6.0.0 Our Superset instance is hosted on Elest.io and last night there was an update to 6.0.1 and all of a sudden our embedded dashboards did not work anymore (403 /api/v1/me/roles/). I tried everything: Gave Public, Gamma and my own custom role literally ALL permissions Tried using curl instead of our product Tried letting Superset provide tokens instead of creating it ourselves Nothing worked. I did a rollback to 6.0.0 and everything works again. Either there is a bug in 6.0.1 or token management has changed drastically || I too had a similar issue . when i change the role to Gamma it works fine . but when i create a new role and assign some embedded dashboard permission I too faced /api/v1/me/roles/ 403 Forbidden error . Then I figured it out . Just assign the can_read CurrentUserRestApi || Thank you! I've been fighting with this for a week. We don't use the Gamma role, but instead have created our own role that has minimal permissions. I couldn't figure out which one was needed. || Yes we too created a new role . Just assign can_read CurrentUserRestApi for that role then 403 role permission error will disappear || Hi Abishek and billbelanger, I'm having the same problem, but I can't find permission can_read CurrentUserRestApi, do you know why? || Hi @worker123-tech It's possible this was only an issue in v6.0.1RC1. I do not see that role in our v6.1.0RC1 environment, but I do still see it in our v6.0.1RC1 deployment (there is a "can read CurrentUserRestApi" and "can write CurrentUserRestApi"). Hope that helps. || I found that the corresponding permission number is 267, so we can look up this number and add it. This works in Superset 6.1.0rc1. However, we need to try multiple times when looking up the permission, because sometimes it won't be found. || Thanks for the info! It looks like the can_read CurrentUserRestApi permission might have been removed in v6.1.0RC1, as I’m not seeing it either. I’ll check our v6.0.1RC1 deployment for the permission and verify. … On Fri, Mar 27, 2026, 5:53 AM Bill Belanger ***@***.***> wrote: Hi @worker123-tech https://github.com/worker123-tech > It's possible this was only an issue in v6.0.1RC1. title: How to sort time series column correctly in Time-Series table ? · apache/superset · Discussion #38937 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Dear developers, In the "Time-Series Table" chart, clicking a column header to sort the data fails to sort numerically if the column has a D3 Number Format applied or is configured to display a Sparkline (in the "Time series columns" setup). There appear to be two underlying causes compounding this issue: D3 Formatting converts to String: When applying formats that append suffixes (e.g., k, M) or fixed decimals (e.g., ,.2f), the frontend table sorts the values lexicographically (alphabetically) rather than numerically. For example, 84k is sorted higher than 2.9M and 150k. Sparkline Object Wrapper: When a metric is configured as a "Time series column" to display a sparkline, the underlying cell value seems to be passed as a complex object/array to render the chart. When a user clicks the column header, the native frontend sorting logic fails to parse the raw numeric value from this object, resulting in broken or random sorting. Expected behavior: Clicking a column header should always sort the rows numerically based on the raw, unformatted backend value of the metric, regardless of the D3 visual formatting applied or whether a Sparkline is rendered in the cell. Current Workarounds (Sub-optimal): Casting the metric to ::FLOAT in the Virtual Dataset SQL does not solve the frontend sorting issue. Therefore, I delete number format in the chart settings doesn't solve the sorting issue. Environment: Superset version: 0.0.0.dev0 - superset_superset docker image pull 4 months ago. When we running docker container, it's so confusing. Database: PostgreSQL
author: BNyamkhuu
comment_excerpt: Hi @BNyamkhuu ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. Both issues you're describing — D3-formatted columns sorting lexicographically and Sparkline columns failing to sort — have been identified and fixed in recent PRs: Column sorting always falling back to the first column (which affects both D3-formatted and Sparkline columns): The root cause was that the sortNumberWithMixedTypes comparator tried to access a data-value prop that didn't actually exist in react-table's row model, causing every comparison to return 0 (equal) and fall back to the default first-column sort. This was fixed in PR #38579 (merged March 19, 2026), which rewrites the sort function to extract the raw numeric value directly from each cell's React props — handling both ValueCell and SparklineCell types [1] [2] .

title: How can i use nginx to deploy Superset? · apache/superset · Discussion #38933 · GitHub

author: FrancescoCastaldi
comment_excerpt: To deploy Superset behind nginx, you'll need to configure both Superset and nginx: Superset Configuration ( superset_config.py ): ENABLE_PROXY_FIX = True PROXY_FIX_CONFIG = { "x_for" : 1 , "x_proto" : 1 , "x_host" : 1 , "x_port" : 1 , "x_prefix" : 1 } Nginx Configuration: :8088/; proxy_set_header Host $http_host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_connect_timeout 300; }"> location / { proxy_pass http :// : 8088 / ; proxy_set_header Host $http_host ; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr ; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for ; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme ; proxy_http_version 1.1 ; proxy_connect_timeout 300 ; } I

title: Issue embedding Apache Superset dashboards behind reverse proxy (public → private VLAN) · apache/superset · Discussion #37376 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hello everyone, I’m facing an issue embedding Apache Superset dashboards in a setup where Superset is hosted on a private server, and I’m hoping someone here may have encountered a similar architecture. Architecture overview: Two servers on the same VLAN / subnet Web server: Publicly accessible NATed and exposed via a domain (e.g. dashboard.example.com) Superset server: Private IP only (not NATed, not internet-facing) Accessible only within the VLAN What we are trying to achieve We want to embed Superset dashboards inside our public web portal. Initial problem When we tried embedding Superset directly, the browser blocked access because: The public website was trying to load Superset content from a private IP Mixed public → private access was blocked by the browser/security policies What we tried next (reverse proxy approach) We configured nginx on the public web server as a reverse proxy: Created a location like: /superset/ This proxy forwards traffic to the private Superset server IP This allowed the Superset UI to load partially. Current issues Static assets / CSS / JS issues (partially resolved) Initially, CSS and JS were broken because Superset was trying to load assets from its original base URL. We added additional nginx location blocks for static assets, which fixed the UI rendering. Embedding dashboards still not working Dashboard embedding results in: Too many redirects, or 404 errors on dashboard routes Behavior is inconsistent depending on the URL and embedding method. Key challenges we suspect Superset generating absolute URLs based on its internal base URL Redirects not respecting the /superset/ path prefix Possible issues with: SUPERSET_WEBSERVER_BASEURL ENABLE_PROXY_FIX X-Forwarded-* headers APPLICATION_ROOT or path-based reverse proxy support Authentication / session cookies not aligning across domains and paths Question to the community Has anyone successfully embedded a non-NATed (private) Superset instance into a public portal using nginx (or another reverse proxy)? Is Superset officially supported behind a path-based reverse proxy (e.g. /superset/)? Are there recommended configurations or workarounds for this kind of network setup? Any guidance, reference architectures, or configuration examples would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance 🙏
author: akaladmin
comment_excerpt: Hi @akaladmin ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. Superset now officially supports running behind a path-based reverse proxy (like /superset/ ) as of version 6.0. For earlier versions, subpath deployments are unreliable due to hardcoded paths and are not recommended for production use. If you’re on 6.0 or newer, here’s what works: Superset config (superset_config.py or env): APPLICATION_ROOT = '/superset' STATIC_ASSETS_PREFIX = '/superset' ENABLE_PROXY_FIX = True PROXY_FIX_CONFIG = { "x_for" : 1 , "x_proto" : 1 , "x_host" : 1 , "x_port" : 1 , "x_prefix" : 1 } Or set SUPERSET_APP_ROOT=/superset and STATIC_ASSETS_PREFIX=/superset as environment variables. This ensures all URLs and static assets use the correct prefix. ENABLE_PROXY_FIX and PROXY_FIX_CONFIG let Superset trust the pro || Hi @dosu , Thank you for your response, I really appreciate it. title: ducklake support · apache/superset · Discussion #33666 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, Duckdb team released a new format : ducklake. https://ducklake.select/ Do you think it is possible to query a ducklake from superset ? I guess it is possible using the duckdb connector. But not sure how to manage authentication to a ducklake metastore stored in duckdb
author: dridk
comment_excerpt: Hi @dridk ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. Superset does not have native, explicit support for the DuckLake format, but you can likely query DuckLake tables from Superset if DuckDB itself can access them. Superset connects to DuckDB using its DuckDB connector, which passes SQL queries directly to DuckDB. If you have set up DuckLake in DuckDB (by installing the DuckLake extension and attaching the catalog), Superset should be able to query any tables that DuckDB exposes, including those managed by DuckLake, as long as the underlying DuckDB SQLAlchemy dialect supports it. There is no special handling or abstraction for DuckLake in Superset—everything depends on DuckDB's capabilities and configuration [ source ]. For authentication to the DuckLake metastore: this depends on the b || Superset does not have a built-in feature to run a one-time setup query automatically after establishing a DuckDB connection. The DuckDB engine spec does not implement any hooks for post-connection setup queries, and there is no configuration option for this in the UI or connection settings. While Superset’s base engine spec provides a get_prequeries method for some databases, this is not used by the DuckDB integration and is generally intended for session-level (not one-time) setup queries reference . If you need to run setup SQL (such as installing extensions or attaching catalogs), you will need to do this manually outside of Superset—either directly in DuckDB or via a script—before connecting Superset to the database. There is currently no supported way to automate this step from withi || Can you configure a .duckdb file beforehand that has the ducklake connected and pass that file in the connection string? Will give this a try later. @dridk @dosu || I've written up what I know about this so far here: https://medium.com/data-analytics-at-nesta/ducklake-on-apache-superset-d1b1b00c222a || I made a fork of duckdb-engine renamed to duckdb-driver and included some code in there to better support ducklake. You can find it at https://github.com/wolkwork/duckdb_driver If you then follow the guide from @danlewis85 its possible to connect to a ducklake! || @N-Clerkx what are the changes in your fork, they are not clear from the README

title: Superset API: 404 Not Found when updating dashboard using access_token + csrf_token (but works with browser session) · apache/superset · Discussion #38869 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Environment Superset version: 6.1.0rc1 Deployment: Docker Authentication: REST API ( /api/v1/security/login ) + CSRF Problem Description I am trying to update a dashboard via the API: PUT /api/v1/dashboard/{pk} ✅ Working Case (Browser / curl copied from DevTools) When I copy the curl request directly from the browser DevTools and execute it, the update succeeds. This request includes: session cookie csrf token proper headers ❌ Failing Case (Programmatic API flow) I tried to reproduce the same behavior using API calls: Login to get access_token : POST /api/v1/security/login Get CSRF token: GET /api/v1/security/csrf_token/ Send request: PUT /api/v1/dashboard/{pk} with headers: X-CSRFToken: Cookie: Content-Type: application/json"> Authorization: Bearer X-CSRFToken: Cookie: Content-Type: application/json ❗ Actual Result 404 Not Found Expected Result Dashboard should be updated successfully (same as browser behavior). Key Observation The same endpoint works when using browser session (copied curl) Fails when using access_token + csrf_token All requests are executed in the same session context (cookie preserved) Additional Notes If Authorization header is removed and only cookie is used → works If using Bearer token → returns 404 instead of 401/403 Question Is there a mismatch between: session-based authentication token-based authentication CSRF validation in Superset 6.1.0rc1? Or is mixing access_token and session cookie unsupported for write APIs? Request Clarification on the correct way to: Authenticate API requests Use CSRF with Bearer token Update dashboard programmatically Thanks!
author: worker123-tech
comment_excerpt: This is a known behavior pattern in Superset. The 404 error doesn't mean the dashboard doesn't exist — it means the authentication context is causing the dashboard to be filtered out by the DashboardAccessFilter before the request is processed. Root Cause : When you call /api/v1/security/csrf_token/ without the Authorization header, the session cookie isn't associated with the authenticated user. This causes subsequent requests to have mismatched authentication contexts. Correct Flow : import requests # 1. Login login_resp = requests . post ( "https://superset.example.com/api/v1/security/login" , json = { "username" : "admin" , "password" : "password" , "provider" : "db" } ) access_token = login_resp . json ()[ "access_token" ] # 2. Get CSRF token WITH Authorization header (critical!) sess || The problem is that using the exact same request flow, calls to /api/v1/theme are authenticated as the admin user, while calls to /api/v1/dashboard/{pk} are instead treated as coming from an anonymous user. Is there any additional header required when calling /api/v1/dashboard/{pk} compared to other endpoints? || @dosubot[bot]

title: How can i change logo "Superset" (APP_ICON)? · apache/superset · Discussion #38820 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, how can I change my APP_ICON inside my app? I already added APP_ICON to superset_config.py and rebuilt and restarted the app, but it still doesn’t work. How can I fix this?
author: FrancescoCastaldi
comment_excerpt: APP_ICON is deprecated in Superset v5+ and ignored when any theme config is present. You need to use the theme system instead. Add this to your superset_config.py : THEME_DEFAULT = { "token" : { "brandLogoUrl" : "/static/assets/images/your_logo.png" , "brandLogoAlt" : "Your App Name" , "brandLogoHref" : "https://your_website" , "brandIconMaxWidth" : 150 , "brandLogoHeight" : "40px" , } } THEME_DARK = { "token" : { "brandLogoUrl" : "/static/assets/images/your_dark_logo.png" , }, "algorithm" : "dark" , } Important : Configure both THEME_DEFAULT and THEME_DARK — if you only set one, the logo will revert to default when users switch modes . Place your logo file at the path specified in brandLogoUrl (e.g., superset/static/assets/images/your_logo.png ), restart Superset, and clear your browser c || How can i put my logo.png froom project's root to the indicated path?

title: superset-init won't go up · apache/superset · Discussion #38808 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I'm experiencing an issue with superset-init that gets stuck in a loop at: Built apache-superset-core @ file:///app/superset-core Prepared 1 package in 19.62s Uninstalled 1 package in 17ms Installed 1 package in 7ms ~ apache-superset-core==0.1.0rc1 (from file:///app/superset-core) Reinstalling the app in editable mode It doesn't proceed further after running build and compose up -d. How can I resolve this?
author: FrancescoCastaldi
comment_excerpt: Hi @FrancescoCastaldi ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. This is a known issue, usually caused by a version mismatch between your Git branch and the Docker image . When using docker-compose-image-tag.yml , your local docker/ folder gets mounted and overrides the container's entrypoints. If you're on master or latest-dev branch but using an older image tag, the bootstrap script may reference tools (like uv ) that don't exist in the older image, causing the loop. Recommended fixes: Match your Git branch to the image version: git fetch --tags git checkout 4.1.1 # or whatever version you're targeting docker compose -f docker-compose-image-tag.yml up Or use the non-dev compose file which avoids these mount issues: docker compose -f docker-compose-non-dev.yml up If the above don't hel || vr. 6.0.0 || Version 6.0.0 has some known Docker initialization issues . Since you're using the default docker-compose.yml which mounts your local code, make sure your Git checkout matches: git fetch --tags git checkout 6.0.0 Then try: docker compose down docker compose build --no-cache docker compose up -d If that still loops, check the init logs to see what's happening: docker compose logs -f superset-init This will show you the actual error. The "Reinstalling the app in editable mode" message is normal — the issue is likely what happens (or doesn't happen) after that step. Can you share the full log output from superset-init ? To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? Good | Irrelevant | Incorrect | Verbose | Hallucination | Report 🐛 | Other || Building Superset frontend in dev mode inside docker container npm error code ENOTEMPTY npm error syscall rename npm error path /app/superset-frontend/packages/generator-superset/node_modules/fs-extra npm error dest /app/superset-frontend/packages/generator-superset/node_modules/.fs-extra-noNkzu9G npm error errno -39 npm error ENOTEMPTY: directory not empty, rename '/app/superset-frontend/packages/generator-superset/node_modules/fs-extra' -> '/app/superset-frontend/packages/generator-superset/node_modules/.fs-extra-noNkzu9G' npm notice npm notice New major version of npm available! 10.8.2 -> 11.12.0 npm notice Changelog: https://github.com/npm/cli/releases/tag/v11.12.0 npm notice To update run: npm install -g npm@11.12.0 npm notice npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /roo || This ENOTEMPTY error is a known issue caused by residual Docker volumes conflicting with npm's rename operations during install. Try these fixes in order: Clear Docker volumes completely: docker compose down -v docker system prune --volumes docker compose up --build If that doesn't work, enable npm prune mode: export NPM_RUN_PRUNE=true docker compose up This triggers a full cleanup of node_modules before running npm install . Fallback — build frontend locally instead of in Docker: Set BUILD_SUPERSET_FRONTEND_IN_DOCKER=false in your .env file, then run npm install and npm run dev directly on your host machine. Let us know if any of these help! To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? Good | Irrelevant | Incorrect | Verbose | Hallucination | Report 🐛 | Other || not working llm boy

author: FrancescoCastaldi
comment_excerpt: This is a known issue caused by residual Docker volumes from previous builds conflicting with npm's rename operations. Simply removing the node_modules folder isn't enough—you need to clean up the Docker volumes as well. Try running these commands: docker-compose down -v docker system prune --volumes Then rebuild: docker compose up --build Note: docker system prune --volumes removes all unused volumes system-wide, so be careful if you have other Docker projects with data you want to preserve. If that's a concern, you can try just docker-compose down -v first to only remove Superset-related volumes. This issue was reported previously with the same ENOTEMPTY error during npm install in the superset-frontend container. To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? title: Cartodiagram plugin: support dynamic perimeter masking and polygon boundary aggregation · apache/superset · Discussion #38692 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Feature request This issue is only about the plugin-chart-cartodiagram . I would like to improve how filtered polygon data is displayed in the cartodiagram plugin, especially when working with administrative boundaries at different levels. Use case 1: dynamic mask outside the selected area When a filter is applied, I would like to have a dynamic mask on the map so that areas outside the selected perimeter are visually faded or hidden. The goal is to make the active area easier to identify, whether: no filter is applied yet; one or more filters are active. This would improve readability and make the current spatial focus clearer in dashboards. Use case 2: dynamic merge of polygon entities Another use case is the dynamic merge of polygon entities on the fly. My dashboard provides 3 filters based on different boundary levels. In some cases, when filtering at a higher level, I do not want to keep displaying all lower-level polygon boundaries. For example: if the filter corresponds to a level 3 boundary, I do not want to continue seeing level 4 boundaries (for example commune/city boundaries), I only want to see the resulting outer perimeter corresponding to the selected level 3 area. In other words, for polygon geometries, it would be useful to support a dynamic aggregation/dissolve behavior so that filtered entities are displayed as a single perimeter instead of multiple internal boundaries. Expected result For the plugin-chart-cartodiagram , it would be useful to support: a dynamic mask around the currently relevant area; a dynamic dissolve/merge of filtered polygon entities to display only the outer perimeter. Additional context I have already started working on these developments in our custom terrestris build based on Superset 5.0.0: https://github.com/terrestris/superset/tree/5.0.0-custom A PR will be proposed soon once the implementation is stabilized. Please let me know whether I should open a dedicated issue for this in addition to this discussion.

Actual behavior API returns empty data: {"count":0,"result":[]} Could anyone help me explain why the Admin user cannot get any dashboard/chart data via REST API?Is this a known issue in version 4.1.2? @dosubot
author: xokok
comment_excerpt: Hi @xokok ! I'm Dosu and I’m helping the superset team. This is a known issue in Superset 4.1.x. The /api/v1/security/login endpoint returns a JWT token but does not set the session cookies that many API endpoints require. Without the session cookie, the API silently returns empty results instead of a proper 401/403 error. The fix : After login, you need to fetch a CSRF token, which will automatically set the necessary session cookies: def login ( self ): url = self . base_url + '/security/login' data = { "password" : "password" , "provider" : "db" , "refresh" : True , "username" : "apiuser" } headers = { "Content-Type" : "application/json" } resp = self . session . post ( url , data = json . dumps ( data ), headers = headers , verify = False ) if resp . status_code == 200 : self . token = || I try as you said, but still return empty data: login successful CSRF token obtained, session established ('response status:', 200) ('response data:', u'{"count":0,"description_columns":{},"ids":[],"label_columns":{"certification_details":"Certification Details","certified_by":"Certified By","changed_by.first_name":"Changed By First Name","changed_by.id":"Changed By Id","changed_by.last_name":"Changed By Last Name","changed_by_name":"Changed By Name","changed_on_delta_humanized":"Changed On Delta Humanized","changed_on_utc":"Changed On Utc","created_by.first_name":"Created By First Name","created_by.id":"Created By Id","created_by.last_name":"Created By Last Name","created_on_delta_humanized":"Created On Delta Humanized","css":"Css","dashboard_title":"Dashboard Title","id":"Id","is_managed || Thanks for following up. This appears to be a known bug in Superset 4.1.x where Bearer token authentication returns empty results even when permissions are correct. First, verify your session cookie is being set properly: # After calling CSRF endpoint, check if cookies are set: print ( "Cookies:" , self . session . cookies . get_dict ()) If the session cookie is present but you still get empty results, there are two workarounds: Option 1: Check Public role permissions Go to Settings → List Roles → Public and ensure it does NOT have can read on Dashboard or can read on Chart permissions. These can interfere with Bearer token authentication . Option 2: Use browser session cookie (most reliable) Since session cookies from browser login work correctly , you can use this approach: Log in to Sup

title: Alerts without screenshots · apache/superset · Discussion #35060 · GitHub

author: datasc24
comment_excerpt: You can configure Superset alerts (event-driven notifications) to send emails with only the dashboard or chart URL and no screenshot by setting the ALERTS_ATTACH_REPORTS flag to False in your configuration. However, for scheduled reports, Superset will still include screenshots in the email—there isn't a built-in option to exclude screenshots from reports and send only the URL. The dashboard URL is always included in the email body, and you can control its format with the WEBDRIVER_BASEURL_USER_FRIENDLY setting. More details are in the configuration file and documentation: see here and here . To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? title: Warm up charts cache via API · apache/superset · Discussion #38718 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: @dosu I need to warm up cache via API if data changed in dataset, and charts were updated my logic GET /api/v1/explore/?slice_id=1 - get form data POST /api/v1/chart/1/data/ - execute query and get query context PUT /api/v1/chart/warm_up_cache - cache When I do request on explore HTTP 403 status is returned My custom role has almost all permissions for charts and data ["ExploreFormdataRestApi", "can_read"], ["ExploreFormdataRestApi", "can_write"], ["ExploreFormdataRestApi", "can_put"], ["ExploreFormdataRestApi", "can_post"], ["ExploreFormdataRestApi", "can_get"], ["ExploreFormDataRestApi", "can_read"], ["ExploreFormDataRestApi", "can_write"], ["ExplorePermalinkRestApi", "can_read"], ["ExplorePermalinkRestApi", "can_write"], ["ExploreRestApi", "can_get"], ["ExploreRestApi", "can_read"], ["ExploreRestApi", "can_write"],
author: rsaleev
comment_excerpt: You don't need the /api/v1/explore/ endpoint at all! The warm_up_cache endpoint handles everything internally. Simplified workflow: Just call the warm up endpoint directly: PUT /api/v1/chart/warm_up_cache Content-Type: application/json { " chart_id " : 1, " dashboard_id " : 123, // optional " extra_filters " : [] // optional } This endpoint fetches the chart config, executes the query with force=True , and stores the result in cache automatically. Why you're getting 403: Your permissions target ExploreFormdataRestApi and ExploreFormDataRestApi , but the /api/v1/explore/ endpoint actually requires "Explore" read permissions — that's a different permission class. Permissions needed for your simplified approach: For /api/v1/chart/warm_up_cache , you need "Chart" write permissions : [ "Chart"  || @dosu GET /api/v1/chart/{chart_id} doesn't return form data, that's why I use /explore payload = { "form_data": form_data, "force": True, "datasource": { "id": form_data["dataset"]["id"], "type": form_data["dataset"]["type"], }, } form_data from previous request TypeError: QueryContextFactory.create() missing 1 required keyword-only argument: 'queries' || The error is because queries is a required field that cannot be auto-generated from just form_data . You need to construct it from the form_data fields. Correct payload structure: # Extract query fields from form_data query = { "columns" : form_data . get ( "groupby" , []) + form_data . get ( "columns" , []), "metrics" : form_data . get ( "metrics" , []), "filters" : form_data . get ( "adhoc_filters" , []), "row_limit" : form_data . get ( "row_limit" , 10000 ), "time_range" : form_data . get ( "time_range" , "No filter" ), "granularity" : form_data . get ( "granularity_sqla" ), "orderby" : form_data . get ( "orderby" , []), "extras" : { "time_grain_sqla" : form_data . get ( "time_grain_sqla" ), } } payload = { "datasource" : { "id" : form_data [ "datasource" ]. split ( "__" )[ 0 ], # or ho || @dosu Can I create query context before chart created and slice_id is defined? Or I must create chart with params and only then update it's query context? || Yes, you can create a chart with query_context in a single API call — no need to create first and update later. The ChartPostSchema accepts query_context as an optional field during chart creation: POST / api / v1 / chart / { "slice_name" : "My Chart" , "datasource_id" : 1 , "datasource_type" : "table" , "viz_type" : "table" , "params" : "{...}" , # your form_data as JSON string "query_context" : "{...}" # your query_context as JSON string } So your workflow can be: Build your params (form_data) and query_context JSON strings Create the chart with both in one POST request Then warm_up_cache will work immediately since query_context is already stored To reply, just mention @dosu . How did I do? Good | Irrelevant | Incorrect | Verbose | Hallucination | Report 🐛 | Other || @dosu I have created manually query_context and chart updated {"datasource":{"id":344,"type":"table"},"force":false,"queries":[{"filters":[{"col":"Variable","op":"IN","val":"speed"}],"extras":{"having":"","where":""},"applied_time_extras":{},"columns":[{"columnType":"BASE_AXIS","sqlExpression":"x","label":"x","expressionType":"SQL","isColumnReference":true},"Group"],"metrics":[{"aggregate":"COUNT_DISTINCT","column":{"advanced_data_type":null,"changed_on":"2026-03-22T11:07:28.809501","column_name":"data_fact","create_on":null,"description":null,"expression":"","filterable":true,"groupby":true,"id":1727,"is_active":true,"is_dttm":false,"python_date_format":null,"type":"DOUBLE PRECISION","type_generic":0,"uuid":"36cd406c-391c-4049-845c-bd913e5b0677","verbose_name":null},"datasourceWarning":fa || This is a known bug in Superset versions 4.1.1-4.1.4 . The error occurs because the code uses list(set(extra_cache_keys)) to deduplicate cache keys, but set() can't hash list values — which are common when using IN filters. Workaround options: Patch the code — In superset/connectors/sqla/models.py , find the line with list(set(extra_cache_keys)) and change it back to just return extra_cache_keys Upgrade Superset — Check if a newer version has fixed this issue Also note: Your filter value should be a list for the IN operator: // Change this: { "col" : " Variable " , "op" : " IN " , "val" : " speed " } // To this: { "col" : " Variable " , "op" : " IN " , "val" : [ " speed " ]} While this isn't the direct cause of the unhashable error (that's the set() bug), the IN operator expects a list/tup || Encountering charts.WarmUpError: unhashable type: 'list' during warm_up_cache. Any idea why this error is occurring, especially with lists in the query context? @dosu … On Sun, Mar 22, 2026, 8:23 AM Rostislav Aleev ***@***.***> wrote: @dosu https://github.com/dosu > I have created manually query_context and chart updated {"datasource":{"id":344,"type":"table"},"force":false,"queries":[{"filters":[{"col":"Variable","op":"IN","val":"speed"}],"extras":{"having":"","where":""},"applied_time_extras":{},"columns":[{"columnType":"BASE_AXIS","sqlExpression":"x","label":"x","expressionType":"SQL","isColumnReference":true},"Group"],"metrics":[{"aggregate":"COUNT_DISTINCT","column":{"advanced_data_type":null,"changed_on":"2026-03-22T11:07:28.809501","column_name":"data_fact","create_on":null,"descrip || issue was in orderby list, third layer of list object was created. Will try to exclude patch later. So it's a payload issues only and lack of payload validations. Faulty params and query_context are in DB now, not good. || @dosu new error raised Error: Referenced columns not available in DataFrame. but chart works fine. I have created it via API and fetched as embedded via SDK. Do I need to patch anything or only suppress error?
