title: "Percentage of total" metric · plausible/analytics · Discussion #365 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: "t would be nice to have a % based view. Currently all the numbers are absolute. It would be nice to know how much % of users got converted. I currently do the mental math in my head, by dividing by the number of unique visits. The same way for "Top Sources", I have concrete numbers, but I would like to know how many % of users came from which site. The same thing for "Top Pages" - with percentages, I can easily see "Ah. 10% of the users visit the support page, and 50% of them go to the pricing page". I could achieve this by making each page a goal, but that's cumbersome." [request via email] With all this filtering we now allow, could be interesting to add a "Percentage of total" metric to the top chart (where we have unique visitors, total pageviews, bounce rate, visit duration). So whatever you click and filter the dashboard by, there on the top you can easily see say Hacker News sent 720 unique visitors which is 25% of your total traffic in this time period. Same for pages, countries, devices, goal conversions and everything else.
author: metmarkosaric
comment_excerpt: How would we feel about this combining with #117 ? i.e. a user picks one of the four metrics at the top to see in graph form (I'm unsure if we actually store the hour by hour data for the last two, I'd have check) and then the same is used as the denominator for these calculations || Could be great combined for sure @Vigasaurus ! Having "percentage of total" in addition to being able to click on a metric and switch the main graph to show that metric would make Plausible so much more powerful. || I'm not sure I understand how these two features combine. If I click on bounce rate, what metric would be shown in the Top Pages report for example? According to the logic it would be pageviews / total_bounce_rate which feels like would be a nonsense metric? It does make sense for when you click on pageviews or visitors but I can't quite imagine how it would work with bounce rate and session length || I imagined that it was less so a combination and more so just that the same user input for selection would apply to both - so it'd still only use views or users as the denominator, but the graph would change with the same clicks but for all 4. It'd definitely be a bit of a big thing to make sure that the edge cases for the percentages get handled - and I don't think it'd actually end up being done by me since I've realized my elixir is too weak for it heh. || Ah OK I see. That sounds good as a feature. If you want you can sketch it out on the frontend with dummy data and I can hook it up to the backend. If you're looking to learn Elixir, @felipesere , @iamvery and myself maintain Elixir koans that will get you up to speed. || Sounds good! I may look into doing something similar for various other medium-ish features - i.e. scaffolding the front-end out and letting someone with stronger elixir top up the backend - since frontend is generally my strong suit anyways (apart from Node backends). Elixir koans that will get you up to speed Cool - I'll look into that || Glad I am not alone on this. I found this discussion because the goals are only absolute. It feels really weird I have the absolute Uniques and Total conversions for the goals, but having to do the ratio of UNIQUE VISITORS manually. And there is soooo much space in the Goals Conversion table. || the conversion rate is already shown on the overview table for quick overview. check our live demo https://plausible.io/plausible.io || Ah, OK. Exactly what I was hoping for :) I guess that's a difference to the self-hosted version then. || ahh i see. you're on the self-hosted version. self-hosted is not updated as frequently as the hosted version which changes daily. think of it as a long term release to help us and help you not need to upgrade things all the time and risk breaking stuff. we stack all the new features and fixes that have been battle tested on the hosted version and release a new self-hosted version twice a year. you can learn more here: https://plausible.io/blog/building-open-source version 1.2 of self-hosting is ready to be beta tested and this one has the feature you're looking for. see #605 and if you're enjoying self-hosted Plausible and finding it useful, please do consider sponsoring the project to give back to the community and to contribute to the long-term sustainability of the project. see details

title: Issue with Geolocation for Internal IPs in Self-Hosted Plausible Analytics · plausible/analytics · Discussion #4984 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi everyone, I'm encountering an issue with my self-hosted instance of Plausible Analytics. I've deployed Plausible within an internal network, and I'm monitoring applications that are also on my company's internal network. These applications are only accessible via VPN. The problem arises when users connect through the VPN. They are assigned internal IP addresses, which Plausible Analytics cannot geolocate since these IPs are internal to our network. This results in a lack of geolocation data for our analytics. I know I can get the information on the internal IP ranges and figure out the location of each of them. Is it possible to use a custom IP range location database that Plausible can consume? This way, we could map internal IP addresses to their respective locations within our network. Has anyone else faced a similar issue or found a workaround for geolocating internal IP addresses? Any suggestions or solutions would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance for your help!
author: joaoaxerb
comment_excerpt: 👋 @joaoaxerb Yes, it is possible to use a custom MMDB file. Please see https://github.com/plausible/community-edition/wiki/Configuration#ip_geolocation_db || Hi! Sorry for the delay on this, I got a bit sick. I'll look into this again once I get better. || @ruslandoga no problems my friend, wishing you a speedy recovery. || 👋 @joaoaxerb I tried using your database in my self-hosted instance (Plausible CE v2.1.5) and I was able to reproduce the problem. I noticed that the current release of Plausible uses locus-2.3.6 and above I used the latest locus-2.3.11 . So, I tried to find where the problem had been fixed and it seems like locus-2.3.7 cannot find "10.72.29.123" and locus-2.3.8 can. There is a note in the changelog for v2.3.8 about some fix so I guess that was what made it work: https://github.com/g-andrade/locus/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#238---2024-05-04 I'm going to open a PR that updates locus to the latest version in Plausible CE and make another release once it's merged. PR: #5104 In the meantime you can try using ghcr.io/ruslandoga/plausible:my-build-update-locus built from ruslandoga#375 . Plausible || Hello @ruslandoga ! I pulled the docker image with your updates. I was able to lookup the IP address via docker exec: Plausible.Geo.lookup("xx.xx.xx.xxx") %{ "city" => "Belo Horizonte", "country" => "BR", "network" => "xx.xx.xx.0-10.72.29.255", "region" => "Minas Gerais" }"> iex(plausible@9fa7d1d23385)2> Plausible.Geo.lookup("xx.xx.xx.xxx") %{ "city" => "Belo Horizonte", "country" => "BR", "network" => "xx.xx.xx.0-10.72.29.255", "region" => "Minas Gerais" } However I noticed some new errors: First, my app won't reconize there is an active visitor on the monitored app: Also, even though locus was able to find the locations, the map remains empty: Docker logs returned: running PlausibleWeb.Endpoint (connection #PID , stream id 1) terminated"> Server: 127.0.0.1:8000 (http) Request: POST /api/ || Ah, right. Plausible assumes a certain structure of the lookup results, like the availability of country iso codes and city geoname ids. I guess the custom MMDBs would need to follow that structure. analytics/lib/plausible/ingestion/geolocation.ex Lines 5 to 20 in 7dd5664 || Hello @ruslandoga ! Sorry for the late reponse, I stoppped working on this project for a while. I finally was able to make it work. Turns out, the CLI 'mmdbctl' was not suitable to work with Plausible. I managed to generate the .mmdb file using Golang scripts and following this project example: https://github.com/maxmind/mmdb-from-go-blogpost Thanks for all the help and support! || Thank you for the update!

title: BigQuery integration / Automatic data export to bucket · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1777 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: To enable data analytics over big data sets, multiple web properties, and easy reporting a connection to BigQuery is super helpful. Google Analytics supports this through Analytics 360. I do not expect you to directly integrate it at this point but offering a regular (daily) csv export to a Google Cloud Bucket (or S3, I guess 😉) would enable this as well. Implementing it this way will also enable other customers to use these exports in more ways than one. Once the data is in that bucket I can query the bucket and push it into BigQuery automatically. Other tools can also do similar things with new data in a bucket. Obviously the implementing a BigQuery connection directly would be preferred for me but I am happy with the csv to bucket path.

It extracts information from Plausible stats API and loads it into any supported CloudQuery destination such as Snowflake, BigQuery, S3 etc. See https://www.cloudquery.io/docs/plugins/sources/plausible/overview || @metmarkosaric there is no plugin like that || @motiejunas looks like the plugin got deprecated: https://community.cloudquery.io/t/cloudquery-integrations-deprecation-notice/972/7 But there is a link to the binary of the plugin in the linked thread.

author: evanlegamer
comment_excerpt: Only API keys for the Stats API can be created through the UI. We currently provision keys for the Sites API manually. It is possible to insert an API key directly in to the database. It needs to have scope sites:provision:* || Sweet! Enjoy Plausible || Ok i succeed to do it I connected to the database and then UPDATE api_keys SET scopes = '{sites:provision:*}' WHERE name = 'Development'; I did the same but got the same error Edit : finally it worked 👨🏽‍💻😁 || Edit: Found the answer in the documentation (Please contact us to get an API key with permissions for the endpoints listed in this document) Is the sites API available on hosted? || Is the sites API available on hosted? Yes it's available Just use your custom url instead of plausible.io when you want to perform requests || Hi, can you share the steps to add API key to the db? Thanks || @evanlegamer do you have any references for how you generated the key? does it have to be generated with the SECRET_KEY_BASE that is set through the env? Also not sure of the key_prefix if that needs to be set || yeah +1 here, If I want to utilize the API on my own self hosted instance, what do I need to do? Can someone help. thanks || I ended up writing a connector to the database directly and perform manipulations I need directly. Pitty we wont get good API access on the open source version but it goes against their business model. || Okay, I had this problem too and managed to solve it using the pointers in this issue, but writing the correct query took a little time, so hopefully I can help someone else to make it quicker. Assuming you want to add the sites:provision:* -scope to an existing api key, make sure you know the key name and use it in the query below: UPDATE api_keys SET scopes = array_append(scopes, 'sites:provision:*') WHERE name='my-key'; Alternatively, if you want to use an api key only for site provisioning (and remove the default access to the stats API), you can use the query by evanlegamer #1460 (reply in thread) : UPDATE api_keys SET scopes = '{sites:provision:*}' WHERE name='my-key'; If you're unsure how/where to run this query, here is a oneliner for the default self-hosted setup. For this to work

title: Feature request: Option to combine stats for pages with and without trailing slashes · plausible/analytics · Discussion #336 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Precheck Please note that this tracker is only for feature requests. Do not use the issue tracker for help or support. Our docs are a great place for most answers, but if you can’t find your answer there, you can contact us . Have an issue with your self-hosted install? You can ask in our community forum . Thanks! Prerequisites I have searched open and closed issues to make sure that the feature has not yet been requested. Feature request Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. My website does not add trailing slashes to the end of each URL (e.g., example.com/hello vs example.com/hello/). Plausible counts them separately. For example, see https://plausible.io/docs.plausible.io/pages How would you like it to work I would like an option to combine the stats for pages with and without trailing slashes Screenshots (If applicable) If applicable, add screenshots to help explain the proposed solution.
author: theodorechu
comment_excerpt: Thanks for the suggestion, I've noticed this myself on https://plausible.io/docs.plausible.io/pages If possible, I think the best solution is to change the website itself to redirect to ensure a consistent URL style. If not then I agree, there should be a way to merge analytics for paths with trailing slash and ones without. || The problem is not with the website itself, the problem is with third party services that manipulate the URLs. For instance, my website has all the endpoints without the final slash, but I submit some URLs of my site to third party services that alter them, and then serve the same URL with a final slash. That's why I think that's very critical to add an option to merge this kind of URLs, to have reliable stats. || Is there an option to set the "pageview url" that's being tracked? That could enable developers to set the url for pageviews to the canonical for example. || @davidsteeb Not at the moment. Makes sense to add that option though || Having the same issue. Many frameworks on the frontend produce URLs without trailing slashes when doing history transitions, whereas if someone visits the URL directly the backend tooling (whether it is Netlify router or Heroku static buildpack) adds the slash. Would be super cool to have some toggle in the settings whether to merge these or not. +1 to that! || Would it be too aggressive to strip trailing slash by default and instead provide an option to preserve it? I might be projecting my own preferences to others but I feel like most people want clean URLs(without trailing slashes) for both their site and analytics. || We should always provide an option, the question is what the default should be. || @ukutaht Sure, would work for me the best. I was not meaning to suggest a solution, just wanted to give +1 to this issue. Thanks for looking into it, any solution would be great! || At least from the SEO perspective, this should be done on the site itself as having two versions of the same page (http or https, www or non-www, / or non-/) can be treated by Google as two different pages which can cause the duplicate content penalty from the search algorithm. Something like this in the .htacess file fixes the trailing slash (most CMS have this sorted out of the box too): RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d RewriteRule ^(.*)/$ /$1 [L,R=301] We could fix this automatically in our reports (combine them both into one) but the issue still remains for the site which should be fixed. (more info: https://ahrefs.com/blog/trailing-slash/ ) || Would definitely be cool from seo perspective. Companies pay a lot of money for technical seo audits and this would be one of the things that is flagged in the audits.

title: How-to track if specific content was viewed by visitor · plausible/analytics · Discussion #2636 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I was looking for a way to measure CTR Click Through Rate on a Call-To-Action image or text, I needed to know if the image/text was visible to the viewer, scrolled into view. Found the Intersection Observer API on https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Intersection_Observer_API Basically set a ID in a div somewhere on the page and trigger a Plausible custom event when this the div comes into view. "> and then... console.log('Tracking CTA'); const options = { threshold: 1, }; function handleIntersection(entries) { entries.map((entry) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { console.log('CTA visible - log event and unobserve'); plausible('ctaVisible'); observer.unobserve(entry.target); } }); } const observer = new IntersectionObserver(handleIntersection, options); observer.observe(document.getElementById("myCTA")); "> console.log('Tracking CTA'); const options = { threshold: 1, }; function handleIntersection(entries) { entries.map((entry) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { console.log('CTA visible - log event and unobserve'); plausible('ctaVisible'); observer.unobserve(entry.target); } }); } const observer = new IntersectionObserver(handleIntersection, options); observer.observe(document.getElementById("myCTA")); The threshold option is for how much of the element that must be visible for the event to trigger, 1=100% This should be in the docs - happy to write a more detailed how-to in that case.
author: mheland
comment_excerpt: thank you for sharing this guide @mheland ! very useful! tagging it here with the word impressions in case someone else searches for this || Thank you! (Two years late, alrite!) @metmarkosaric - tried Plausible with React, it works like a dream, we track events on a SPA, had Claude plug it in for us, effortless the whole thing. || that for sharing, I am trying to achieve the same! Is ctaVisible the name of the custom event then? @mheland || Yes, that's the custom event name, ctaVisible, define in plausible('ctaVisible'); And add it to Plausible Admin, Goals

title: Issue with Plausible Self-Hosted Tracking in Phoenix LiveView (Page Navigation Without Full Refresh) · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5359 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I have self-hosted Plausible and everything is mostly working fine. However, I’ve encountered a problem when integrating it with a Phoenix LiveView application. The issue is: when a user refreshes the page manually, the page view is correctly recorded in Plausible. But when users navigate between pages inside the app (using LiveView links, without a full page reload), Plausible does not register the new page views and has no way of knowing where the user currently is on the website. All JavaScript options are enabled. Is there a recommended way to make Plausible aware of navigation changes in a LiveView environment where full page reloads don't happen? Any guidance would be much appreciated! Thanks a lot!

title: Clash between ON CLUSTER in table DDL queries and Replicated engine · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5356 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt:
The Replicated database engine essentially makes (database-scoped, eg no user mgmt) DDL queries run with ON CLUSTER ... implicitly. However, on top of this it also disallows database-queries having explicit ON CLUSTER ... in queries... Which makes migrations fail with eg Loading plausible.. Starting dependencies.. Starting repos.. Running migrations for Elixir.Plausible.Repo Running migrations for Elixir.Plausible.IngestRepo warning: code block contains unused literal "Migration adds a ALIAS columns needed to keep DB schema and api
property naming in sync to reduce overhead in code.
" (remove the literal or assign it to _ to avoid warnings) └─ lib/plausible-0.0.1/priv/ingest_repo/migrations/20240502115822_alias_api_prop_names.exs: Plausible.IngestRepo.Migrations.AliasApiPropNames (module) ** (FunctionClauseError) no function clause matching in Ch.RowBinary."-inlined-decode_string_decode_rows/5-"/5 The following arguments were given to Ch.RowBinary."-inlined-decode_string_decode_rows/5-"/5: # 1 "version 24.9.3.128 (official build)). (INCORRECT_QUERY) (version 24.9.2.42 (official build))
" # 2 [:u16, :i64, :string, :u64, :u64] # 3 [] # 4 [["ode: 80. DB::Exception: There was an error on [chi-dev-app-main-0-1", 14650, 8017287196693246000, "e: 80. DB::Exception: It's not initial query. ON CLUSTER is not allowed for Replicated database. (IN", 6869189296659779395, 2891356424380962129], ["chi-dev-app-main-0-0", 9000, 80, "Code: 80. DB::Exception: It's not initial query. ON CLUSTER is not allowed for Replicated database. (INCORRECT_QUERY) (version 24.9.2.42 (official build))", 0, 0], ["chi-dev-app-main-0-1", 9000, 80, "Code: 80. DB::Exception: It's not initial query. ON CLUSTER is not allowed for Replicated database. (INCORRECT_QUERY) (version 24.9.3.128 (official build))", 1, 1]] # 5 [:string, :u16, :i64, :string, :u64, :u64] (ch 0.2.5) lib/ch/row_binary.ex:608: Ch.RowBinary."-inlined-decode_string_decode_rows/5-"/5 (ch 0.2.5) lib/ch/query.ex:183: DBConnection.Query.Ch.Query.decode/3 (db_connection 2.6.0) lib/db_connection.ex:1433: DBConnection.decode/4 (db_connection 2.6.0) lib/db_connection.ex:801: DBConnection.execute/4 (ch 0.2.5) lib/ch.ex:87: Ch.query/4 (ecto_sql 3.11.1) lib/ecto/adapters/sql.ex:519: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query!/4 (elixir 1.16.0) lib/enum.ex:1700: Enum."-map/2-lists^map/1-1-"/2 I'm mildly reluctant to make this a feature request, since it's probably niche especially for selfhosted, so... yeah, not too sure how to proceed besides doing the migrations by hand.

title: No credentials sent to AWS SES · plausible/analytics · Discussion #3757 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I'm having the following problem: no mail gets delivered through Amazon SES. I'm not using the "mail" service in docker-compose.yml, and I correctly configured the env variables, as i can read them correctly with Application.get_env(:plausible, Plausible.Mailer) . After sending the test email with Plausible.Mailer.deliver_now(email) , i receive this: 15:59:49.064 [warning] Description: 'Server authenticity is not verified since certificate path validation is not enabled' Reason: 'The option {verify, verify_peer} and one of the options \'cacertfile\' or \'cacerts\' are required to enable this.' {:error, {:permanent_failure, 'email-smtp.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com', "530 Authentication required\r\n"}} Reading some other issues and answers, it seems that the warning is not a problem... but the 530 is. Also, in the AWS console the credentials show up as "Never used", so i guess they are not sent while opening the connection with the SMTP. Any advice?
author: mister-px
comment_excerpt: I have the same issue. I investigated the Bamboo.SesAdaptor path, but couldn't figure out how to make it work. I don't believe it ships with the default Bamboo mailer, maybe it should if we can't make SSL through AWS SMTP interface work? 10:50:06.232 request_id=F7bG50NcjTNC_dYAAAvR [warning] Description: 'Server authenticity is not verified since certificate path validation is not enabled' Reason: 'The option {verify, verify_peer} and one of the options \'cacertfile\' or \'cacerts\' are required to enable this.' || same. still. || #4149 (comment) describes how to make Plausible v2.1.1 work with AWS SES. Make sure you use the new SMTP adapter: plausible-conf.env MAILER_ADAPTER = Bamboo.Mua || hi @ruslandoga . i really appreciate you're trying to help, but that thread doesn't help dummies like me. || If you are having problems with the proposed solution, please start a discussion and someone might try to help with your Synology setup :) Maybe https://chatgpt.com/share/eecb2228-f959-4072-ac27-a758fb490102 would help. || @ruslandoga well this really does look like the right thread. thanks for the chatgpt thread. i think i've got everything set up now as per the other thread; .exs file, new volume, extra config path - but still no email sending. maybe it IS an issue specific to Amazon SES. @mister-px , did you get it to work? The error i'm getting is: [warning] Description: ~c"Failed to assert middlebox server message" and Reason: [missing: {:change_cipher_spec, 1}] . Maybe this is specific to Synology and you're right i should open a specific thread topic for that, but it looks like an Amazon issue. Any advice? || Please change your extra config to this: import Config config :plausible , Plausible.Mailer , ssl: [ middlebox_comp_mode: false ] || YES!!! that did it, can't thank you enough! || Please change your extra config to this: import Config config :plausible , Plausible.Mailer , ssl: [ middlebox_comp_mode: false ] @ruslandoga Where can I find this config file? || 👋 @dhairyathedev It's a file you need to create yourself, mount it into the Plausible container, and then point to it with EXTRA_CONFIG_PATH env var: https://github.com/plausible/community-edition/wiki/Configuration#extra_config_path _

title: v3.0.0 · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5318 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Warning Please use 3.0.1 patch that contains fix for #5319 The highlights of this release are: Create teams, manage users and permissions Scroll depth, engagement time and (reworked) time on page metrics Segments feature: group filters into persistent, named presets Upgrade Update the image used for plausible compose.yml diff --git a/compose.yml b/compose.yml index f5a0a7e..3e03415 100644 --- a/compose.yml +++ b/compose.yml @@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ services: start_period: 1m plausible_events_db: - image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3.3.102-alpine + image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.12-alpine restart: always volumes: - event-data:/var/lib/clickhouse @@ -27,12 +27,14 @@ services: nofile: soft: 262144 hard: 262144 + environment: + - CLICKHOUSE_SKIP_USER_SETUP=1 healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget --no-verbose --tries=1 -O - http://127.0.0.1:8123/ping || exit 1"] start_period: 1m plausible: - image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.5 + image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v3.0.0 restart: always command: sh -c "/entrypoint.sh db createdb && /entrypoint.sh db migrate && /entrypoint.sh run" depends_on: and restart the containers console $ docker compose up -d This will boot up the new version of the app. Changelog The following changes have been made since 2.1.5: Added Ability to sort by and compare the exit_rate metric in the dashboard Exit Pages > Details report Add top 3 pages into the traffic spike email Two new shorthand time periods 28d and 91d available on both dashboard and in public API Average scroll depth metric Scroll Depth goals Dashboard shows comparisons for all reports UTM Medium report and API shows (gclid) and (msclkid) for paid searches when no explicit utm medium present. Support for case_sensitive: false modifiers in Stats API V2 filters for case-insensitive searches. Add text version to emails Autogenerate text emails #4674 Add acquisition channels report Add filter is not for goals in dashboard Excluding goals in dashboard #4983 Add Segments feature Support ["is", "segment", [ ]] filter in Stats API Time on page metric is now sortable in reports Plausible tracker script now reports maximum scroll depth reached and time engaged with the site in an engagement event. These are reported as sd and e integer parameters to /api/event endpoint respectively. If you're using a custom proxy for plausible script, please ensure that these parameters are being passed forward. Plausible tracker script now reports the version of the script in the v parameter sent with each request. Add support for creating and managing teams owning multiple sites Introduce "billing" team role for users Introduce "editor" role with permissions greater than "viewer" but lesser than "admin" Support behavioral filters has_done and has_not_done on the Stats API to allow filtering sessions by other events that have been completed. time_on_page metric is now graphable, sortable on the dashboard, and available in the Stats API and CSV and GA4 exports/i

title: Resetting user password using the database · plausible/analytics · Discussion #819 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: The reset password method was not working for an account as the email provider was rejecting the emails. I updated the email address by changing it in the database, so that the password reset link can be send (other email provider had no issues). author: bhavin192
comment_excerpt: #736 you'd need to combine a bit of the steps here, but the building blocks are there. You'd need to use the get_by() and then the set_password()` || I stumbled upon this issue since I couldn't get the email server to work, and thus, couldn't get the reset password to work. Posting this here for future me and other people: find your main plausible instance name by doing docker ps run docker exec -it plausible_plausible_1 /app/bin/plausible remote (for me, the name was plausible_plausible_1 ) run Plausible.Repo.get_by(Plausible.Auth.User, email: "youremail@domain.com") |> Plausible.Auth.User.set_password("yourpassword") |> Plausible.Repo.update || This worked perfectly. || Not a fan of this because on Railway, there's no way to execute arbitrary commands to a given Docker instance. Instead, I ended up just getting the SMTP server to work; it's not hard to do it with FastMail anyways. That said, if you don't know your email or if it's a bad one - you're kinda screwed. || I stumbled upon this issue since I couldn't get the email server to work, and thus, couldn't get the reset password to work. Posting this here for future me and other people: Plausible.Auth.User.set_password("yourpassword") |> Plausible.Repo.update`"> 1. find your main plausible instance name by doing `docker ps` 2. run `docker exec -it plausible_plausible_1 /app/bin/plausible remote` (for me, the name was `plausible_plausible_1`) 3. run `Plausible.Repo.get_by(Plausible.Auth.User, email: "youremail@domain.com") |> Plausible.Auth.User.set_password("yourpassword") |> Plausible.Repo.update` Works even after a year! 🙌 Thanks || If it helps anyone else, in case you've forgotten your email as well: you can run Plausible.Repo.all(Plausible.Auth.User) . || This just helped me out, thanks!

comment_excerpt: 👋 @Tony-MF I'm not sure if there is an API endpoint for this, but you can probably do this by connecting to the running container (if you are using containers) and calling a few functions. $ cd hosting $ docker compose exec -ti plausible bin/plausible remote import Ecto.Query iex> alias Plausible.{Site, Repo, Auth} # this code is untested iex> add_members = fn role, emails, sites -> Repo.transaction(fn -> sites = Site |> where([s], s.domain in ^sites) |> Repo.all() users = Auth.User |> where([u], u.email in ^emails) |> Repo.all() for site sites = ["example.com", "dummy.site"] iex> emails = ["user@gmail.com", "another@gmail.com"] iex> add_members.(:admin, emails, sites)"> iex > import Ecto.Query iex > alias Plausible . { Site , Repo , Auth } # this code is untested iex > add_members = fn ro || This code works 💯 Thanks a lot 🙏 I just had to open the interactive terminal with: sudo docker-compose exec plausible bin/plausible remote || this is not currently supported. there's a feature request thread here #1350 || Here is an updated code that skips users who already have access to the site (otherwise it returns an error and stops). import Ecto.Query iex> alias Plausible.{Site, Repo, Auth} add_members = fn role, emails, sites -> Repo.transaction(fn -> sites = Site |> where([s], s.domain in ^sites) |> Repo.all() users = Auth.User |> where([u], u.email in ^emails) |> Repo.all() for site where([m], m.user_id == ^user.id and m.site_id == ^site.id) |> Repo.one() if membership do IO.puts("User #{user.email} already has access to #{site.domain}. title: How to test missing data from imported data of Google Analytics? · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5213 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, after migration in manual mode I got plausible working, what I did: Manual backup postgres/clickhouse Restore backup clickhouse (in my case only postgres has problem) Running clean postgres with plausible Backup new postgres to get schema Stop Plausible/postgres Get only data and apply the data from new schema for what is required NOT NULL Start postgres Ingestion of only the new data (the FK is turned off and on) Start again plausible Everything is working but only a site with old google analytics don't show, I checked on clickhouse for the imported_* table and get the data. I want to know how tables I need to check for the site_id or the SQL used in the filter for "All time" to run local to check what table is missing the connection to start working again: site_id for this one is 11: clickhouse - client USE plausible_events_db; select count ( * ) from imported_ * where site_id = 11 ; # have data select count ( * ) from imported_custom_events where site_id = 11 # none select count ( * ) from ingest_counters where site_id = 11 # none select count ( * ) from sessions_v2 where site_id = 11 # none select count ( * ) from sessions_v2_backup where site_id = 11 # none \c plausible_db plausible_db = # select imported_data from public.sites where id = 11; imported_data -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- { " id " : " d562e386-5fe1-4edb-a983-19e6e8ee68ea " , " source " : " Google Analytics " , " status " : " ok " , " end_date " : " 2023-06-18 " , " start_date " : " 2019-11-08 " } ( 1 row) The list of tables I have manually imported for postgres: COPY public . users COPY public . sites COPY public . site_memberships COPY public . api_keys COPY public . goals COPY public . google_auth COPY public . salts COPY public . setup_help_emails COPY public . setup_success_emails Any direction where I need to check or the SQL to run to check why the historical don't show?
author: webysther

title: Users are not correctly being counter? Seems like they are being double counted. · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5176 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I'm self-hosting plausible on my game https://bulletz.io , which is also hosted on https://poki.com/en/g/bulletz-io . On both sites I've verified that the plausible sdk is only initialized once and is not sending duplicate events several times on both sites, but I'm still seeing my "daily users" double the amount of users who are eligible to complete events. For example, today I have "26k" users and the most an event has been completed, which is immediately fired upon users getting the payload, is 13k. Thest rest follow suit with slightly below 13k. I'm fairly sure the real users are 13k, and that they're getting double counted. I verified immediately after midnight that every country has exactly a multiple of 2 "users", so I could sort of figure out that from the fact that the more niche countries would all have exactly 2 users that this was the case. Now the hard part; I cannot for the life of me figure out why this is the case. The SDK is initialized once as follows: this.plausible = Plausible({ domain: args.plausible.domain, apiHost: args.plausible.provider, hashMode: false, trackLocalhost: false, }); and I track events: this.plausible?.trackPageview({ url: path, }, { props: this.customProperties }); My understanding of plausible tells me that this shouldnt result in the behavior I'm seeing; yet it does. Any advice or thoughts? Has anyone else struggled with something similar? Its not the end of the world, but it does make it really hard to see my funnels in a meaningful way.

title: Option to display page titles · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1450 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: The 'Top pages' report can only show URLs, which makes it a bit cumbersome to use for reporting back to the rest of the organisation. For a simpel report on e.g. our Top 25 pages, I'd have to manually look up and copy 25 page titles into an Excel. Would it be possible to add a toggle switch or menu option to show 'Titles' instead of 'URLs'?
author: rvlak
comment_excerpt: thanks @rvlak ! haven't heard this request before. could be interesting to experiment by allowing to for instance click on "page url" header to change it to show "page title" instead. or allow the export of url + title in our CSV. is there any specific reason that you're reporting by page title rather than the url? || Hi @metmarkosaric Thanks for your kind reply! I compile quarterly reports on our online outreach and share them with my colleagues, and I know from experience that reports with real page titles are much easier for them to grasp / read than having to interpret sometimes cryptical URLs :-) || makes sense, thanks for sharing the details! we will consider this but i cannot make any promise on if or when it will be done. we are a small team with limited development resources so need to focus on most frequently requested features first. thanks for understanding! || Sure, I fully understand! || I would recommend a page report that is based on page title instead of URL. I serve one URL with a vary header that responds with content in different languages. Differentiating by title would allow me to see which languages are most popular. It can also take the place of dealing with hashes since hash updates also usually update the title. || I'm with you @rvlak || Please consider adding this. The use-case is the same as above. || Do some people also need both? Or is it a choice between URL vs title? Meaning, should we collect and surface BOTH the title and url, or would it be ok to just collect one or the other depending on user choice? || HI @ukutaht Ideally we would like to have both (similar to Google Analytics), e.g. with a toggle switch or menu option to show 'Titles' instead of 'URLs' || Yes I agree that would be perfect

title: Support SRI for the plausible.js tracker · plausible/analytics · Discussion #380 · GitHub
author: ukutaht
comment_excerpt: How would we want to handle versioning for the tracker script here? Would we introduce a proper versioning system such that we'd just publish the hashes for specific versions? And similarly for the different script types? I honestly think that with the nature of the script and the versions (hash, outbound etc, not actual revisions) of it available, proper SRI is difficult to do well without forcing lots of overhead on users to update script versions when it changes and update the SRI hash anytime they want to change script type or version. If there's a different idea of how to do this well that'd be cool, but I think SRI is unsuitable for plausible in its current state. The potential volatility and need for user intervention anytime there's an important tracker script change (think vulnera || Hmm yeah that's a valid point. At this point it's so useful to be able to push an update and have it take effect once everyone's browser cache expires. I agree that adding an SRI is not practical at the moment. || I've deployed Plausible with SRI. Please notify me here or through another mean, such as a mailing list, when you change the served script. I would prefer it, though, if I could reference a versioned script from you in order to have more time to upgrade the version and the hash. The versioning can be optional for those who want SRI. Being able to use SRI is another nice differentiator of Plausible Analytics over Google Analytics. (I have no problem with hosting the script myself, but I would still need a way to be informed when a newer version is available.) || I agree with this 100%. Would be great to have versioning for those who want SRI! || Here is a related discussion: #2414 I think plausible should turn the tracker into a versioned library. That library can then be used via either NPM (avoiding adblockers) or via a wrapper script ( || An old discussion, but are there any updates on this? We're also looking to add SRI to our Plausible script. Would it be a good idea to download the script and host it on our own server?

title: Self hosted plausible on MicroK8S + wordpress + proxy, geolocation all in the same country · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5103 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I'm running a self-hosted instance for plausible, it monitors WordPress websites with the plugin. To circumvent ad blockers, I use the nginx proxy setup with some modifications to the plugin ( plausible/wordpress#228 ) Now in the dashboard all my visitor seem to come from one country? any idea? Bart
author: semanticfire
comment_excerpt: 👋 @semanticfire Maybe NGINX doesn't set x-forwarded-for header? Please double check your proxy_set_header in NGINX configuration. || This is what I have (.*?)).js { proxy_pass https://stats.netage.nl/js/plausible.$target.js?ver=2.1.4; proxy_set_header Host stats.netage.nl; }"> location ~ /js/stats.(? (.*?)).js { proxy_pass https://stats.netage.nl/js/plausible.$target.js?ver=2.1.4; proxy_set_header Host stats.netage.nl; } and location = /api/event { proxy_pass $plausible_event_url; proxy_set_header Host example.com; #proxy_buffering on; proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host; } || to be clear the x.x.x.x is not the ip of the k8s server, its from the web server. is there an nginx running in the docker container of plausible? || is there an nginx running in the docker container of plausible? No :) || I've implemented a solution on the ingress, need to check tomorrow if now other countries popup, keep you posted || Okay it was indeed the double proxy, nginx [wordpress server] -> nginx-ingress -> plausible the problem was with the nginx-ingress which cleared and modified the headers causing the wordpress server to provide the IP. I've followed this guide to update the nginx-ingress https://marcolenzo.eu/configure-microk8s-nginx-ingress/ short version, add the following to the data part of the ingress configmap use-forwarded-headers: "true" So follow up question would be, where to document this?? || Nice! So follow up question would be, where to document this?? I think it's OK as a solution in this discussion. Nobody really reads the documentation or our CE wiki :)

title: Could not check origin for Phoenix.Socket transport · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5075 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: @ruslandoga Opening a new discussion as per your comment Story Just dumping information here as I'm not sure where I messed up the configuration. I have my domain managed by cloudflare with a zero trust tunnel pointed to nginx proxy manger with web sockets enabled and ssl validated with letsencrypt. I am able to get to the dashboard when I point the proxy to https://192.168.1.444:5479 . But the data from the site isn't showing up which I believe has to do with the error in the docker logs. Could not check origin for Phoenix.Socket transport. Orgin of request: https://192.168.1.444:5479 This happens when you are attempting a socket connection to a different host than the one configured in you config files. For example, in development the host is configured to "localhost" but you may be trying to access it from "127.0.0.1". To fix this issue, you may either: 1. update [url: [host: ...]} to your actual host in the config file for your current environment (recommended) 2. path the :check_origin option when configuring your endpoint or when configuring the transport in your UserSocket module, explicitly outlining which origins are allowed: check_origin: ["https://example.com", "//another.com:888", "other.com"] Architecture cloudflare zero trust nginx proxy manger ubuntu server Configs Using the base community compose with docker override as per docs. BASE_URL = https://analytics.foo.com SECRET_KEY_BASE = " 11pldLJ1kxqalr1ahucy/+sx1poD0iuyqhwxT32pXsw= " # generated for discussion HTTP_PORT = 80 HTTPS_PORT = 443 services: plausible: ports: - 5478:${HTTP_PORT} - 5479:${HTTPS_PORT} services: plausible_db: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: always volumes: - db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"] start_period: 1m # networks: #- caddy plausible_events_db: image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3.3.102-alpine restart: always volumes: - event-data:/var/lib/clickhouse - event-logs:/var/log/clickhouse-server - ./clickhouse/logs.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logs.xml:ro # This makes ClickHouse bind to IPv4 only, since Docker doesn't enable IPv6 in bridge networks by default. # Fixes "Listen [::]:9000 failed: Address family for hostname not supported" warnings. - ./clickhouse/ipv4-only.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/ipv4-only.xml:ro # This makes ClickHouse consume less resources, which is useful for small setups. # https://clickhouse.com/docs/en/operations/tips#using-less-than-16gb-of-ram - ./clickhouse/low-resources.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/low-resources.xml:ro ulimits: nofile: soft: 262144 hard: 262144 healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget --no-verbose --tries=1 -O - http://127.0.0.1:8123/ping || exit 1"] start_period: 1m #networks: #- caddy plausible: image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.4 restart: always command: sh -c "/entrypoint.sh db createdb && /entrypoint.sh db migrate && /entrypoint.sh run" depends_on: plausible_db: condition
author: LucasZapico
comment_excerpt: 👋 @LucasZapico If you access your dashboard at https://192.168.1.444:5479 then your BASE_URL needs to be https://192.168.1.444:5479 and not https://analytics.foo.com || Doesn't that throw an error on build because Plausible can't get a valid cert for an IP? Also I'll rebuild my container with you suggestion and see if the issue is resolved. Also noticed that the dashboard is not receiving data. IDK. Thank you for the response || Just tried it and it's gives the expected error; ....elixir error"> Runtime terminating during boot ({#message=> ....elixir error || That's right. You would need to use something else to generate the certificates. The default setup doesn't assume using Zero Trust tunnels so you need to find your own workarounds to make it work. || Hey just wanted to circle on this. It is my understanding that using proxies(zero trust) should have no effect on this. The setup is the same as pointing a domain to an IP on Linode, Hetzner, etc. The problems would persist as the app(Plausible) is using :. I'm unclear on how you are supposed to handle this. Thank you || I suggest to keep things simple and use the default setup, i.e. a FQDN to reach the dashboards instead of Zero Trust tunnels.

title: Constant, unexplained "Could not check origin for Phoenix.Socket transport" errors in log · plausible/analytics · Discussion #4561 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I've set up plausible to run with the reverse proxy being hosted on a second machine that accepts connections from outside of our firewall using the Apache reverse proxy settings suggestions. This works fine except I get a constant stream of errors like these: [plausible] | 08:56:59.081 [error] Could not check origin for Phoenix.Socket transport. [plausible] | [plausible] | Origin of the request: http://devserver.ourdomain.com:8000 [plausible] | [plausible] | This happens when you are attempting a socket connection to [plausible] | a different host than the one configured in your config/ [plausible] | files. For example, in development the host is configured [plausible] | to "localhost" but you may be trying to access it from [plausible] | "127.0.0.1". To fix this issue, you may either: [plausible] | [plausible] | 1. update [url: [host: ...]] to your actual host in the [plausible] | config file for your current environment (recommended) [plausible] | [plausible] | 2. pass the :check_origin option when configuring your [plausible] | endpoint or when configuring the transport in your [plausible] | UserSocket module, explicitly outlining which origins [plausible] | are allowed: [plausible] | [plausible] | check_origin: ["https://example.com", [plausible] | "//another.com:888", "//other.com"] [plausible] | [plausible] | 08:57:59.139 [error] Could not check origin for Phoenix.Socket transport. ... These errors come consistently at 1 minute intervals even if - as far as I can tell - nothing is accessing the plausible instance. Accessing http://devserver.ourdomain.com:8000 directly in a web browser also does not seem to trigger this warning. As I stated above, everything seems to be working as intended, I can register and start tracking. But this is making the log very noisy, so any advice on how to resolve this would be most welcome. Configuration used: plausible-conf.env: BASE_URL=https://subdomain.ourdomain.com SECRET_KEY_BASE=REDACTED TOTP_VAULT_KEY=REDACTED DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:REDACTED@plausible_db:5432/plausible_db docker-compose.yml: services: plausible_db: # Plausible v2.1.1 was tested against PostgreSQL versions 15 and 16 # https://github.com/plausible/analytics/blob/v2.1.1/.github/workflows/elixir.yml#L21-L32 image: postgres:16-alpine restart: always ports: - 5432:5432 volumes: - db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=REDACTED plausible_events_db: image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3.3.102-alpine restart: always volumes: - event-data:/var/lib/clickhouse - event-logs:/var/log/clickhouse-server - ./clickhouse/clickhouse-config.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logging.xml:ro - ./clickhouse/clickhouse-user-config.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/users.d/logging.xml:ro ulimits: nofile: soft: 262144 hard: 262144 plausible: image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.1 restart: always command: sh -c "sleep 10 && /entrypoint.sh db createdb && /entrypoint.sh db migrate && /entrypoint.sh run" depends_on: - pl
author: kris-sigur
comment_excerpt: 👋 @kris-sigur Please share your configuration with real domain names. In short, the error means that Plausible receives WebSocket connection requests from an origin that is different from what you have set in BASE_URL. It's meant to protect from Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) attacks. These errors come consistently at 1 minute intervals even if - as far as I can tell - nothing is accessing the plausible instance. Something is :) || @kris-sigur did you figure this out? I am running into this error as well. || @kris-sigur I encountered the same problem as you, have you solved it? || 👋 @chienxx @LucasZapico Please start new discussions with your compose.yml , .env (with real domain names), docker compose exec plausible env , and docker compose logs . And please take some time to explain exactly what's happening in your case.

title: Deleting spurious data · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5046 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I'd like to delete some spurious data from the Clickhouse DB, specifically: A specific URL on a specific day. https://plausible.adamu.jp/retirewiki.jp?period=day&keybindHint=C&date=2024-10-15&filters=((is,page,(/w/index.php))) Does anybody have any hints where to start with this or potential gotchas. The background is that when upgrading to the version of Plausible that moved the exclusions filters server-side, I didn't set it up correctly, so ended up with thousands of entries to index.php that I'd like to remove.
author: adamu
comment_excerpt: 👋 @adamu You might be able to connect to ClickHouse and delete these records there: $ cd plausible-ce $ docker compose exec plausible_events_db clickhouse client -d plausible_events_db :) delete from events_v2 where timestamp between ' 2025-10-15 ' and ' 2025-10-16 ' and pathname = ' /w/index.php ' ; :) delete from sessions_v2 where start between ' 2025-10-15 ' and ' 2025-10-16 ' and pathname = ' /w/index.php ' ; Please double check these queries before executing. Maybe with a corresponding SELECT statement. Note that the dates here are GMT, and the date in URL is local for the site timezone. || Hi @ruslandoga , thanks for the help! There is no pathname column on sessions_v2 , and deleting the records from events_v2 seemed to blank out the session_v2's entry_page column for the corresponding records. However, I managed to clean it up by deleting the records from sessions_v2 where entry_page = '' . delete from events_v2 where pathname = ' /w/index.php ' ; delete from sessions_v2 where entry_page = ' ' ; Now no random spike in the history :) https://plausible.adamu.jp/retirewiki.jp?period=all Thanks again!

title: How do you get plausible CE 2.1.4 to create a ssl certificate when no proxy is used · plausible/analytics · Discussion #5016 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Just installed a fresh installation of Plausible CE 2.1.4, but even though the wiki says that the proxy is not needed and Plausible is able to manage ssl certificates automatically, no ssl is generated. How can you get Plausible CE 2.1.4 go generate a ssl when no proxy is used?
author: sideskift
comment_excerpt: 👋 @sideskift The certificates are generated automatically if HTTP_PORT and HTTPS_PORT are set to 80 and 443 respectively (step 3 from quickstart ). If you share your compose.yml and docker compose logs we might be able to find what went wrong. || I checked back on the server a few hours later, and then the ssl certificates was in place. Could be a cron job that had not yet executed? So is there a command I can execute on the container that generates the ssl certificates, in case I experience something similar in the future? || The certificate is generated on startup. There is a certificate renewal job but it's unrelated to the initial certificate issuance. There is a command to force certificate renewal (in case of a leak) you could call, but it won't help here -- it has already been called on startup if the configuration is correct. || Do you know the command to force certificate renewal? And do you mind sharing it? :-)

author: GeorgBNM
comment_excerpt: 👋 @GeorgBNM You can delete the data for that date range from your "normal" Plausible installation and then it would probably work. || I see. So, will you solve this “problem” with a future update? || I don't know of any plans to change the exports/imports logic right now. Sorry. || Thanks for your effort, @ruslandoga ! But working like this, the import logic seems not feasible, right? Best, Georg || I agree. But I think the idea was that the "native stats" (i.e. recorded by Plausible) are dominant and the imports are secondary and are only for the data that was recorded before switching to Plausible, like as if someone had been using Google Analytics since 2013 and then switched to Plausible in 2024, they would be able to import 2013 till 2024. The ability to fill in the holes in data that has already been recoded by Plausible (in your case that's all data after 2022-07-26) was probably left out due to possible complications. But that's just my opinion and doesn't represent the position of the core team :)

author: kevinsdi
comment_excerpt: thanks for reporting that @kevinsdi ! sounds weird! everything seems to be working fine here and we have no other reports of this. could it be that you're running some type of adblocker that's blocking our website? || It is not working from Chrome, Safari, Brave Browser, Firefox, and it is not depends on any browser extension, but its working in Tor Browser || And it worked 5-7 days ago with no problems || Sounds like your DNS resolver is the problem then (it applies for the whole OS, thus all browsers would be affected, except Tor which forcibly changes it). I'd say you should try changing the DNS server your computer is using from the current to something like Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 or Quad9's 9.9.9.9 and see if it works then (it should, in theory). You'd need to find instructions to change the DNS server based on your OS/browser of choice though (I believe all the major browsers to offer DNS over HTTPS now, and that's usually easier than changing the OS-level setting). || The problem is in my VPN)) It's working fine without VPN 🤷‍♂️

title: Scoped API tokens · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1767 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I was a bit surprised when setting up an API key that it has global permission across all sites and endpoints. We have one site where the dashboard and stats are public and another where they're private. It'd be nice to be able to share the API key for the site with public stats without worrying about it also having access to the private stats, but the only way I can think to do that would be by creating multiple accounts (which is a pretty big hack!). For public sites, it also seems like the API key is pretty harmless, but I'm not sure and I'd worry that eventually an API key would become read/write, even if it's a purely read-only API now (is it? I'm not sure). Anyhow, it'd be nice to scope a key per endpoint, so that we could safely share a key for the breakdown API on a particular site, say. This is just a small detail, but something to think about if/when something like this ever comes along might be to add the site name to the key itself. For example, instead of a key like: asdflakdjfkalsdfkjasldfajsdfklasjfkalsjfkslajf You could have: google.com-asdfalsdkfjasdflajskdfjaksldfkjaskdfj Anyhow, just some ideas! Thanks for the great tool.
author: mlissner
comment_excerpt: Thanks @mlissner . I anticipated this point but I cut the scope for this to get the API out quicker. We actually store a scope field but at the moment it's always something like stats:read:* or sites:provision:* . The idea was that in the future it could be like stats:read:sitea.com,siteb.com or whatever. This isn't implemented but it's like 70% there on the backend. As you can see the stats api is meant to be readonly and it will stay that way forever. We have a separate Events API and Sites API that have write permissions. So yeah, sorry for not having it yet. It will get implement at some point but I can't tell when. || Love it, that's great, thanks.

title: Proxying Plausible through Svelte / Vercel · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1553 · GitHub

I saw those guides but I'm not able to setup the whole thing using svelte deployed to Vercel. Can you please provide documentation for this usecase ?
author: rdupz
comment_excerpt: hi! cname feature is not effective anymore. you can still use the cname and many of our subscribers still do but proxy is a much better solution. adblockers now crawl the web looking for cnames and can block them when discovered so cname doesn't give you any better stats than our default script. adblockers cannot do the same with the proxy. we have several standalone proxy instructions and these can be set up on its own and it does not matter what hosting provider or tech stack you are using. cloudflare is an easy one to start with. see https://plausible.io/docs/proxy/guides/cloudflare || by the way not sure if you saw it but we also have vercel instructions here https://plausible.io/docs/proxy/guides/nextjs || Is it possible to do a proxy with webflow? I've looked around online but I can't find any details on how to set it up. || i don't think webflow allows you much flexibility in terms of server access etc so cloudflare is probably the easiest one to use with webflow: https://plausible.io/docs/proxy/guides/cloudflare || It was actually quite simple using cloudflare worker. Thanks || you're welcome! and glad to hear that!

title: Netlify redirects and Geo-location · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1733 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: A few days after running a self-hosted instance of Plausible for a website hosted on Netlify, I'm seeing that the list of countries seems to contain just six countries (USA, Germany, SGP, Australia, Brazil, Russia). I know that visitors from other countries have been visiting the site. Why aren't the other countries appearing in the list? My thinking is I'm proxying Plausible through Netlify as described in the Plausible documentation Netlify's regular CDNs are in exactly those six countries, based on their answer to a question on their forum. Therefore, it could be that when Netlify redirects, it sends the IP address of the CDN server, instead of the user's IP address Plausible's documentation for self-hosting as well as for proxying through Nginx seems to suggest that headers like X-Forwarded-For are important to have the remote client IP forwarded to the Plausible server. Therefore, my hypothesis is: Unless headers are forwarded alongwith the Netlify redirect rules, the geo-locations will be restricted to the countries where Netlify's CDN servers are located? Is there a way to test this? Does this reasoning make sense? Has someone experienced (and fixed?) this issue? The self-hosted Plausible dashboard does show a non-trivially high number of Unique Visitors. Maybe this is because Plausible uses a combination of User IP and User Agent for determining if a visitor is unique?

title: Is there a max allowed spend on Plausible? · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1728 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I'm wondering what happens with Plausible if there is malicious traffic on a page (that is reloaded ad libitum). Will the spend grow exponentially or is there a way to cap it?
author: Pixelshaped
comment_excerpt: Hi @Pixelshaped ! There is no way to cap the traffic but you will never be charged extra for an occasional traffic spike. There are no surprise fees and your card will never be charged unexpectedly. We haven't experienced any situation like the one you describe yet either as we do our best to exclude any non human / bot traffic by default. Suppose new higher traffic levels become your new standard and your page views exceed your subscription plan for two consecutive months. In that case, we will contact you and ask you to upgrade your subscription plan to accommodate your new traffic levels using our self-serve platform. After we contact you, you will have a week to make a decision. You can decide to continue with a higher plan or to cancel your account at that point. || Thanks! || you're welcome!

title: Plausible vs Google Analytics: differences in most viewed pages (numbers and time) · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1727 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I just made the switch from GA to Plausible (thank you), and I'm now comparing numbers (februari 2022) to report properly to my team about what this switch means for our statistics. Some numbers are quite identical, but some do really differ a lot. What stands out most is the difference in viewed pages and time on page. The list for GA is quite different than for Plausible, and the time on site is almost absurd long. Can someone explain this, so that I can account for this in my team? Thanks!
author: ingejanse
comment_excerpt: hi @ingejanse ! and thanks for the kind words! pageviews looks pretty similar. analytics tools work differently so you can rarely expect identical results no matter how many different providers you test. we recommend you try out our proxy too in case you'd like to count visitors that may use privacy-friendly browsers or adblockers extensions. for time on page, the difference comes from the different way we define the metric. in GA bounced visitors are included in the time on page metric but all set to 10 seconds time on page while in Plausible we exclude bounced visitors from the time on page calculation. Neither of these approaches are 100% accurate (Plausible would show higher than the actual time on page, GA would show lower than the actual time on page) as in both cases it's not possib

title: Tracking both internal & outbound clicks · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1719 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I'm going through the Plausible documentation right now, and I'm a bit confused. The post on tracking outbound links tells me to change the tracking script, but wouldn't that mean the internal tracking wouldn't work anymore? I'd like to track both internal clicks and external link clicks through the global script. I'm using the self-hosted plan, by the way.
author: janoschherrmann
comment_excerpt: All of the script variants (except for manual) work as additions or like extra plugins - i.e. they add functionality only, while the core functions continue to work the same. Manual mode is the only one which turns off automatic pageview tracking. || Ok so when I want to use the normal script and track external links at the same time, I just include both scripts, right? || No - I think calling it a plugin was a bit misleading - just loading the plausible.outbound-links.js will track both the default pageviews from the regular script, and will listen for outbound links. You don't need to include the script several times for each extra function. Similarly, for example, loading just the plausible.outbound-links.exclusions.js will do all of the functionality from the normal script, the outbound link tracking, and the ability for you to specify URL exclusions. (Also, the script extras order doesn't matter in the path either - i.e. you could use plausible.exclusions.outbound-links.js as well, for example). || Ah, perfect. Thank you for your help!

title: Utm parameters ignored · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1056 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Precheck Prerequisites I have searched open and closed issues to make sure that the bug has not yet been reported. Bug report Describe the bug UTM parameters not being collected Expected behavior When visiting links with utm parameters I'd expect them appear in the dashboard. Screenshots Events are being sent with paramters: But not showing up: Environment (If applicable): Self Hosted OS: Linux Browser Chrome Version Latest
author: andrejj
comment_excerpt: UTM parameters only show up in the UI if they start a session. Try hitting that page with UTM tags in a pageview that's the first in a session. You can start a new session by: Waiting 30 minutes Using a different browser Using a browser extension to manipulate the X-Forwarded-For header in order to change the IP address that Plausible uses. || Ok, I retried after few hours and they appeared. Thank you, this solved it. Maybe mentioning this in the docs would make sense. || What's the reasoning for this behaviour? If a user visits my site, has a look around, maybe signs up to a newsletter and then I email them, upselling them on a product I think they're interested in (don't worry I don't do this...). I'd want the link in the email to have UTM params and for those to be tracked so I could see my nefarious actions were working. But with this logic if they come back within 30mins it won't be tracked. || The fact that the referral sources are counted only when they start a new session on your site prevents external domains such as a payment gateway that the user is being taken through to show up in the referrers list. || But a payment gateway wouldn't set UTM params in order to redirect back to your site.

title: "Address already in use" warning in docker container · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1702 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Past Issues Searched I have searched open and closed issues to make sure that the bug has not yet been reported Issue is a Bug Report This is a bug report and not a feature request, nor asking for self-hosted support Describe the bug Hi, first of all thank you for this great tool. I am having some issues with my self-hosted plausible instance. Plausible does not register any page views, no matter with what browser. I have followed every step from: https://plausible.io/docs/troubleshoot-integration#keep-seeing-a-blinking-green-dot My script is correctly proxied (using this method) to plausible.io/js/plausible.js and it loads fine when I open my page. But for some reason plausible does not show anything. I have looked into podman-compose logs and found this: Application: Listen [0.0.0.0]:8123 failed: Poco::Exception. Code: 1000, e.code() = 98, e.displayText() = Net Exception: Address already in use: 0.0.0.0:8123 (version 21.3.2.5 (official build)). If it is an IPv6 or IPv4 address and your host has disabled IPv6 or IPv4, then consider to specify not disabled IPv4 or IPv6 address to listen in element of configuration file. Example for disabled IPv6: 0.0.0.0 . Example for disabled IPv4: :: 2e1fd6003e3d 2022-02-21 22:53:26.905 UTC [1] LOG: database system is ready to accept connections 92ea6954fa07 Starting dependencies.. 036d84d3ccfc 2022.02.21 22:53:30.332078 [ 49 ] {} Application: Listen [0.0.0.0]:9000 failed: Poco::Exception. Code: 1000, e.code() = 98, e.displayText() = Net Exception: Address already in use: 0.0.0.0:9000 (version 21.3.2.5 (official build)). If it is an IPv6 or IPv4 address and your host has disabled IPv6 or IPv4, then consider to specify not disabled IPv4 or IPv6 address to listen in element of configuration file. Example for disabled IPv6: 0.0.0.0 . Example for disabled IPv4: :: 036d84d3ccfc 2022.02.21 22:53:30.365335 [ 49 ] {} Application: Listen [0.0.0.0]:9009 failed: Poco::Exception. Code: 1000, e.code() = 98, e.displayText() = Net Exception: Address already in use: 0.0.0.0:9009 (version 21.3.2.5 (official build)). If it is an IPv6 or IPv4 address and your host has disabled IPv6 or IPv4, then consider to specify not disabled IPv4 or IPv6 address to listen in element of configuration file. Example for disabled IPv6: 0.0.0.0 . Example for disabled IPv4: :: 92ea6954fa07 Starting repos.. 92ea6954fa07 Running migrations for Elixir.Plausible.Repo 036d84d3ccfc 2022.02.21 22:53:30.366247 [ 49 ] {} Application: Listen [0.0.0.0]:9004 failed: Poco::Exception. Code: 1000, e.code() = 98, e.displayText() = Net Exception: Address already in use: 0.0.0.0:9004 (version 21.3.2.5 (official build)). If it is an IPv6 or IPv4 address and your host has disabled IPv6 or IPv4, then consider to specify not disabled IPv4 or IPv6 address to listen in element of configuration file. Example for disabled IPv6: 0.0.0.0 . Example for disabled IPv4: :: 92ea6954fa07 Running migrations for Elixir.Plausible.ClickhouseRepo 92ea6954fa07 Migrations successful! 92ea6

title: ?period=last-month · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1707 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Could be nice to have a way to redirect the dashboard to the view of last month automatically. It can now be done using https://plausible.io/plausible.io?period=month&date=2022-01-23 but would be better with something like https://plausible.io/plausible.io?period=last-month so it always updates to the correct month automatically. (via email)
author: metmarkosaric

title: Per-anchor stats grouped by page · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1699 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: "Is there any way of getting per-anchor analytics on a page? e.g. a breakdown by blahblah.html#first blahblah.html#second. I really would love the ability to see results grouped by page, and then be able to go into those and see which anchors were being visited within that page. Without the grouping it makes it harder to see trends right now" ( via twitter )
author: metmarkosaric
comment_excerpt: For more context, per-anchor stats are supported this way: https://plausible.io/docs/hash-based-routing With this enabled, all page visits are separate by anchor.

title: Unauthenticated CORS enabled endpoints · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1698 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: "I’d like to display the real-time visitors, and the number of visitors in the past 7 days. It’s currently set up as a static website, so I can’t do API calls on a backend without changing a lot of infrastructure code. It would be cool if there were some way to query these datapoints from javascript. I know that for example updown.io has CORS enabled endpoints for public projects. I think it would be cool if you would add that to Plausible as well." (sent via email)
author: metmarkosaric

title: curl to plausible service is always returning either 302 or 429 responses · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1689 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I have deployed the plausible in k8s. Clickhouse, Postgres also running in k8s. Once plausible is up and running, when I tried to curl plausible service from click house or postgres pods I am always getting either 302 or 429 responses. it never return 200 OK. This is expected behavior ?? 302 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 302 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 302 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 429 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 429 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 429 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 429 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 429 root@clickhouse:/# curl -s -o /dev/null -w ''%{http_code}'' http://plausible.plausible.svc.cluster.local:80 302
author: devopssambareddy

title: Overview of used "monthly pageviews" quota per site · plausible/analytics · Discussion #1688 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: It would be great to have an overview of how much of my weekly "pageviews" quote is used per domain. I'm talking about the quota (shown at https://plausible.io/settings ) and not the "TOTAL PAGEVIEWS" field of a stats page (since this does not count events).
author: mschoeffmann
